r At full charge, fires 3 rows of 5 tears, resulting in 15 tears.

anr Mutant Spider: Shoots 7 tears instead of 3. No further decrease in fire rate.
r Shoots 5 tears instead of 3. Decreases fire rate by a little.

r Damage is increased by a flat +5 instead of a normal Polyphemus boost. Decreases fire rate by a little.
Tractor Beam: All tears are fired along the beam, but some may be hard to see as they overlap each other.

nrChocolate Milk: The size and damage of tears shot depends on how far Chocolate Milk is charged.

Tainted Azazel: Although Tainted Azazel's Brimstone beams do not have a damage bonus, they also do not have the normal damage penalty and still deal double damage to enemies that have been sneezed on.

Glitter Bombs: Has a chance to drop a pickup or trinket each time it is used.
Gnawed Leaf: Activating Kamikaze will cancel out of the statue state, but Isaac will not be damaged by the resulting explosion.
Host Hat: Allows unlimited uses without consequences.
Mars: Isaac is invincible during the dash, allowing for unlimited uses with careful timing.
Nancy Bombs: Gains the effect of a random bomb synergy each time it is used.
Pyromaniac: Allows unlimited uses and heals without consequences.
Each use will heal Isaac for nr one full / r half a red heart.
Scapular: Damage from the explosion will trigger Scapular's effect, allowing Isaac to trigger on-damage effects infinitely between rooms.
Sigil of Baphomet: Isaac can chain-kill enemies without taking explosion damage while the shield is active.

Schoolbag: The cool down before the player can use Kamikaze! again is eliminated when switching items. Depending on how fast the player can push the buttons, Kamikaze! can be used multiple times during Isaac's invincibility frames.
Tainted Forgotten: Explodes on Tainted Forgotten’s body, meaning that it is safe to use infinitely, so long as Tainted Soul is not within the blast radius.

collectibles.desc_raw: The next shot is replaced with a beam#It deals 24x Isaac's damage over 0.83 seconds
synergies: 4.5 Volt: Allows the item to be charged and used multiple times in a room, which can help with dealing extra damage in enduring fights like most endgame bosses.
9 Volt: Can be used once every room, and more frequently in Challenge Rooms.
Broken Remote: Since item charges aren't expended until Isaac chooses a direction to shoot, the player can teleport across the floor indefinitely.

Hemoptysis: Enemies hit by the sneeze will gain a curse that increases the power of the laser by 2× and +3 flat damage, on top of the 2× base damage already applied by Shoop da Whoop. Enemies who die to the laser under the curse will damage and spread the curse to nearby enemies.

Ipecac: Increases the damage of the beam greatly, as Ipecac's +40 flat damage boost is added straight to Shoop Da Whoop whithout any damage decrease unlike Brimstone. This basically allows Isaac to kill most normal bosses in 1 shot.

collectibles.name: Dr. Fetus
desc_lines: ↓ x0.4 Fire rate multiplier
Isaac shoots bombs instead of tears
Those bombs deal 10x Isaac's tear damage
If that results in over 60 damage, they instead deal 5x damage +30
collectibles.desc_raw: ↓ {{Tears}} x0.4 Fire rate multiplier#{{Bomb}} Isaac shoots bombs instead of tears#{{Damage}} Those bombs deal 10x Isaac's tear damage#If that results in over 60 damage, they instead deal 5x damage +30
synergies: Apple! / Euthanasia / Tough Love: Damage multiplier may be applied to the non-flat damage without a visual difference.
nr Euthanasia bombs do not retain the instant kill ability.
r Euthanasia bombs retain the instant kill ability.
Bob's Bladder: Bombs from Dr. Fetus leave creep, allowing for extra damage.
Bobby-Bomb / a Homing Tears: Bombs home in on enemies.
Bomber Boy: Bombs explode in a large cross-shaped pattern.
Bomber Boy + Cricket's Body / The Parasite / Polyphemus / Scatter Bombs: The subsequent bomb or bombs explode in a cross-shaped pattern.
Blood Bombs: Bombs leave behind a pool of red creep.
Brimstone / Mom's Knife: Isaac continuously fires bombs while charging the beam/knife.

Brimstone Bombs: The activation rate of the Brimstone lasers is random and dependent on Luck. The chance is 11% at 0 Luck, increasing by 3% per luck. This maximizes at 100% at 29.67 luck and minimizes at 0% at -3.67 luck. The beams of Brimstone Bombs can push fired bombs, making this item powerful but hazardous.
Butt Bombs: Bombs inherit the Butt Bomb effects.
r The activation rate is random and dependent on luck. The chance is 20% at 0 Luck, increasing by 3% per luck. This maximizes at 100% at 26.67 luck and minimizes at 0% at -6.67 luck.
Unlike normal Butt Bombs, this synergy cannot heal Brownie, Clog, Colostomia, Dangle, or Dingle. Both the explosion and the cloud effect deal full damage.
Poisonous Tears : Fires Bob's Curse bombs.
Cricket's Body: The bombs split outwards into 4 tiny bombs, which detonate almost immediately.
The split bombs deal a flat nr 21 damage / r 35 damage.
r Increases range instead of decreasing it.
Cricket's Body + The Parasite: Bombs split outwards into 4 Cricket bombs and 2 Parasite bombs, which detonate and split a second time into the other type, for a total of 23 explosions.

C Section: C Section has priority. Does not apply Dr. Fetus' tears down. The fetuses drop small bombs when they touch an enemy. The bomb's damage follows Dr. Fetus' damage calculation, based on half of the fetuses damage. A fetus can drop only one bomb.

The fetuses' bombs do not benefit from Isaac's bomb effects such as Hot Bombs or Bobby-Bomb. However, they do benefit from Isaac's tear effects and have appearance of the corresponding bombs. For example, Hot Bombs from Fire Mind, Bobby-Bomb from Homing Tears. As such, Cricket's Body, The Parasite and Polyphemus leave a subsequent bomb or bombs, and Mysterious Liquid leaves a creep.
C Section + Ipecac: The fetuses explode before dropping any bomb.

Dr. Fetus: Each additional copy of this item, such as from Diplopia / Crooked Penny, causes Isaac to fire one additional bomb in a side by side spread up to a maximum of 16 bombs. This maximum can’t be surpassed by other multi-shot effects such as 20/20.
Eye of Belial: Bombs get piercing. No more further effect.
r Bombs deal 2.5x piercing damage.
Fire Mind / Hot Bombs: The bombs leave behind a fire that damages enemies on contact after exploding, and bombs themselves deal anr 1 / r points of 16 damage on contact.

Diplopia + Restock or Greed mode: Restock will allow shop items to replenish indefinitely, so by having multiple Steam Sales by the methods above, infinite items may be purchased. Be careful not to pick up Missing No., which will remove the Steam Sales and will likely replace many items with Breakfast.
Each additional Steam Sale will further reduce the price of all items in the shop, down to a minimum of 1 cent.

collectibles.name: Technology
desc_lines: Isaac shoots lasers instead of tears
collectibles.desc_raw: Isaac shoots lasers instead of tears
synergies: Haemolacria: Fires arcing Haemolacria tears that release Technology lasers in all directions upon impact.
Brimstone: Overrides Technology.
a The Technology laser coils around the Brimstone beam, granting a 1.5x damage multiplier, which can stack with other multipliers such as Magic Mushroom's 1.5x damage multiplier.
r Technology's damage multiplier will be overridden if Isaac has the powered up version (from picking up Brimstone twice or using Sulfur with Brimstone).
Epic Fetus: Fires lasers in all directions upon impact of a missile.

Eye of the Occult: The laser becomes dark purple and automatically fires at a controllable crosshair. The crosshair behaves nearly identically to the one created by Marked (including in its synergies and interactions with other items) with the exception that lasers often arch towards the crosshair rather than traveling towards it directly and any other projectiles affected by Eye of the Occult that are not lasers (such as from Eye of Greed or Fate's Reward) will chase the crosshair and hover above it for the remainder of their range.
Technology + Tech.5/Technology 2: Tech.5 and Technology 2 lasers are also affected by the crosshair but cannot create the crosshair on their own.
Fire Mind: Laser lights enemies on fire but loses the ability to explode enemies.
The Inner Eye / Mutant Spider: Isaac shoots multiple lasers with a decreased rate of fire.
Lachryphagy: Isaac shoots a short-range laser that will form a Lachryphagy tear, which has piercing.

The Ludovico Technique: The laser becomes a large ring that is controllable and looks like a Tech X shot. Enemies inside the ring without touching it are not hurt. The tears stat is ignored and the damage is dealt very rapidly at a rate equivalent to a 1 tear delay. nr Shot speed is ignored.
Marked: Isaac can fire lasers at the crosshair.
Mom's Eye: Fires a laser from the back of Isaac's head as well as the front.
Monstro's Lung: Several additional lasers fire in random directions. (One beam will always fire straight forward as normal.)
r Fires a barrage of small lasers.
Pop!: Laser loses its unlimited range, but spawns eyeball tears at the end of the laser.
Proptosis: Doubles damage with no damage-decreasing effect on the laser.
anr Doubles the damage stat, the beams deal 2x damage at point blank range but it rapidly decreases with distance, down to 0.1 from about 8 tiles away.
r The beams do 6x damage at point blank range but decreases to 0.1 damage at about 8 tiles away.
Spirit Sword: Sword becomes a lightsaber. Surrounds Isaac with a laser on every swing. Enemy projectiles are deflected by both normal and charged attacks.
Tech X: Both fire at the same time.
r Fires a Brimstone ring coiled by a laser. Hitbox is bigger with no damage increase.
Technology: Adds an additional laser for every extra copy of the item up to a maximum of 16, with no downgrade to damage or tears.
Technology 2: Equips two lasers that can be fired at the same time. As Technology is always fired from the right eye, it is not affected by the halved normal attack rate.
r Technology is affected by the fire rate multiplier. Holding Stapler, Lead Pencil or playing as Cain will allow Isaac to angle shots with momentum again.

collectibles.name: Chocolate Milk
desc_lines: Chargeable tears
Damage scales with charge time, up to 4x
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Chargeable}} Chargeable tears#{{Damage}} Damage scales with charge time, up to 4x
synergies: 20/20 / The Inner Eye / Mutant Spider: Fires multiple charged shots.
Cricket's Body / The Parasite / Compound Fracture: Since split tears tend to hit the same target the main tear hits, this can allow for much higher damage.
The Forgotten: When charging the club, instead of being thrown, the club grows bigger in size and damage.

Tainted Azazel: The beam takes longer to fully charge, but can be released at any point during its charge. Sneezing on enemies fills half the charge meter and can be immediately released for high damage.
Godhead: Charging the shots increases the size and damage of the aura.
r Charging the shots increases the size of the aura, but not its damage.
Guppy: Firing quickly generates lots of Blue Flies, which can lead to much higher damage than firing tears alone.

Haemolacria: Increased tear delay. Completely charged shots and their burst tears are larger and deal higher damage than normal. Chocolate Milk also allows low-damage Haemolacria shots to be fired rapidly.
Haemolacria + Tech X: rings are created where the Haemolacria shot splashes. The Tech X rings do full damage even on minimum charge shots that were fired rapidly.
Immaculate Heart: Orbiting tears' damage does not depend on the charge of Chocolate Milk, so Isaac can shoot low damage tears rapidly to produce a large number of orbiting tears which do full damage.
Ghost Pepper / Bird's Eye: Fires' damage does not depend on the charge of Chocolate Milk, so Isaac can shoot low damage tears rapidly to fire full damage flames frequently.
Parasitoid / The Mulligan: Flies and spiders generated deal full damage regardless of charge, allowing Isaac to shoot low damage tears to generate flies and spiders rapidly which do full damage.

nrIsaac's Tears / Sad Bombs / Tammy's Head: The size and damage of all generated tears correspond to the current charge level of Chocolate Milk. Isaac's Tears will nearly instantly recharge while charging a Chocolate Milk shot.
r Charge level does not affect generated tears. Isaac's Tears recharge only when a shot is fired.

Ipecac: Increased tear delay. Charged shots deal massively increased damage, as the +40 damage from Ipecac is run through the damage multiplier of Chocolate Milk. It also allows low-damage Ipecac shots to be fired rapidly.

Lachryphagy: Rapidly firing small, uncharged tears can cause the fed tear to grow into a gigantic tear that will still deal fairly low damage while feeding tears with charged tears will result in higher-damage tears.
r Charged and uncharged fed tears deal similar damage.
Mom's Wig: Spiders rapidly spawn while charging.
r Spiders spawn only when a shot is fired.
Monstro's Lung: Reduces Isaac's current tear delay by 20%, allowing Monstro's Lung to charge faster. Each tear from Monstro's Lung is also a charged Chocolate Milk shot, massively increasing damage.
r Tear damage, along with the number of tears fired is dependent on charge time.
Polyphemus: Increased tear delay. Massively increases the damage of each shot, and charged tears decay in damage much slower as they pierce as a result.
Sinus Infection: Booger damage is influenced by the damage of the individual tear, so fully-charged Chocolate Milk shots can result in very potent damage over time.
Technology 2: Damage dealt by the laser is equal to Isaac's current charge, leading to constant high damage from a safe distance.
r The laser deals 13% of Isaac's damage regardless of charge, tears are affected by Chocolate Milk as normal.

synergies: Abyss: When Isaac uses Abyss to absorb The Common Cold it turns into a green locust that poisons enemies.
Compound Fracture/Cricket's Body/Haemolacria/The Parasite: Chance for tears and split tears to be poisonous. If the main tear is poisonous, the split tears are too.
Dr. Fetus/Epic Fetus: Chance for bombs/explosions to become poisonous like Bob's Curse.
Finger!: Finger can also apply the poison effect.
Lachryphagy: If a poison tear is fed, all burst tears are poisonous. If a normal tear is fed, the burst tears are not poisonous.
The Ludovico Technique: The tear flickers green, capable of poisoning enemies when it does.

collectibles.name: The Parasite
desc_lines: Tears split in two on contact
Split tears deal half damage
collectibles.desc_raw: Tears split in two on contact#Split tears deal half damage
collectibles.unlock: Pick up any 2 of the following items in a single run: Bob's Rotten Head, Dead Cat, Cricket's Head, and Tammy's Head (any 2 items that have the "dead" item tag)
synergies: Angelic Prism: All tears split by the prism will burst into split tears when hitting obstacles.
Azazel: Two beams that deal full damage are fired perpendicular to the main beam at the end of the laser.
Brimstone: Two beams that deal full damage are fired perpendicular to the main beam at the closest point of intersection with an enemy or obstacle.
C Section: Fetuses fire short-ranged tears constantly while in contact with an enemy.
Compound Fracture: Both tear split effects happen simultaneously.
Cricket's Body: Two groups of this item's four tears, creating eight additional miniature tears on every point of impact instead.
Flat Stone: Every time tears bounce, burst tears spawn.
Holy Light / Other similar tear modifiers: As the effect is applied before the split, both split tears will retain it.
Jacob's Ladder: Split tears can spawn sparks.
Lachryphagy: Fed tears split into 12 tears. When tears are minimally fed and hit an obstacle, they will split into 5 Lachryphagy burst tears and Parasite burst tears.
The Ludovico Technique: Repeatedly spawns miniature tears when on top of an enemy, greatly increasing damage.
Mom's Knife: Knife splits into two smaller, perpendicular knives that deal full damage on hitting an enemy.
My Reflection: Split tears boomerang back to Isaac, making it easier to hit enemies with them.
Piercing tears: Split tears form for each hit as the tears pass through the enemies.
Spectral tears: When tears pass through solid objects, like rocks, they will split, resulting in many tears shooting out from every rock the original tear passed through.
Technology: Laser splits into two perpendicular beams that deal full damage upon hitting an obstacle or enemy.
Tiny Planet: The split tears will continue to orbit Isaac, increasing damage potential.
Tractor Beam: The split tears will be forced directly forward, impacting whatever caused the first tear to split. This increases damage immensely.
If all shots connect, effective damage will be multiplied by 2 + log2(damage) rounded up, with the maximum increasing as range increases, with a max of 4x at Isaac's base range.
Trisagion: When beams hit obstacles, the beam will split; the split beams can split again when hitting other obstacles.

collectibles.desc_raw: Isaac's tears are replaced by a throwable knife#{{Damage}} The knife deals 2x Isaac's damage while held and caps at 6x damage at 1/3 charge#Charging further only increases throwing range#Damage reduces to 2x when returning to Isaac
synergies: 20/20 / Conjoined / The Inner Eye / Mutant Spider: Isaac holds only one, but shoots multiple knives at their typical spread.
Apple! / Tough Love: The knife deals increased damage sometimes based on luck.

Brimstone / Sulfur: Shooting the knife also shoots a barrage of knives that goes across the screen, with each knife doing 80% (r 2×) of Isaac's damage; the extra knives do not return. The longer the charge, the more knives are added. This effect does not work with Azazel's default mini brimstone. The brimstone-knives do not benefit from A Lump of Coal.
Compound Fracture: The knife creates bone shards while in contact with an enemy.
C Section: Fetuses hold small knives that spin around them slowly, causing extra damage.

Diplopia / Crooked Penny: Each additional copy of the item will grant an extra knife that fires slightly diagonally from the original one, up to a maximum of 16. Picking up more than 16 knives won't fire more knives but will still modify the angle at which they're shot.
Epic Fetus: Each explosion shoots 10 knives in a circle from the point of impact.
Euthanasia / Little Horn: The knife can sometimes instantly kill enemies or deal extra damage to bosses.
Eye Sore: Will occasionally release 1-3 extra knives when shooting Mom's knife.
Fire Mind: Still applies burning, but no longer explodes.
Flat Stone: Knife bounces as it flies away from Isaac, each bounce causing Isaac's tear damage.
Fruit Cake: The benefits of Fruit Cake still occur, but are short-lived due to the high damage of Mom's Knife.

a+ The Forgotten: +4 damage. The knife will attach to The Forgotten's bone club, forming a scythe. Increases the size of both the melee swing and the bone throw, and allows a single melee swing to deal damage to an enemy twice instead of just once.
Ghost Pepper: The flames can shoot from Isaac while holding the fire button.
r The flames only shoot when the fire button is released.
nr Guppy: Knife hits will rarely spawn a blue fly.
r Knife hits will frequently spawn a blue fly.
Holy Light: Holy Light can trigger on both held and thrown knives.
Ipecac: Knife turns green and poisons monsters that hit it. Removes Ipecac's damage increase.
a+nr Adds a flat +4 damage instead of Ipecac's +40. Knife turns green and poison monsters that hit it.

r Adds a flat +2 damage and the knife explodes on contact, once per throw. Will not explode while held without throwing and while coming back to the Player after being thrown. Knife turns green and poison monsters that hit it.
r Ipecac + Technology: Not recommended. Isaac will always take explosion damage when throwing the knife.
Invulnerability: Even though the knife cannot be fired, the knife will deal damage while Isaac is invincible.
Jacob's Ladder: The knife will sometimes generate sparks when hitting an enemy.
Lost Contact: The knife will block bullets, even when held (similar to Trinity Shield but has a smaller blocking area).

collectibles.desc_raw: {{Battery}} Automatically charges the first bar of active items#{{Battery}} Fully recharges the active item on pickup
synergies: 2-bar items: Fills the entire gauge in a single room, effectively granting the opportunity for the item to be used every room.

a+ 3-bar items: Quadruple rooms and L-shaped rooms give 2 bars after clearing them, allowing one free use of the item in those rooms if the gauge is full upon entering them. It can also be used at the beginning of a Boss Challenge Room to get the full charges back after beating both bosses.
AAA Battery + 3-bar items: Same as the 2-bar item synergy without AAA Battery.
a+ 4-bar items: In a Challenge Room, if the item is used during the first wave, it will get all its charges back at the end.
AAA Battery + 4-bar items: Same as the 3-bar item synergy without AAA Battery.
Jacob's Ladder: Increases arcing distance of sparks.
Keeper: The Wooden Nickel and the 9 Volt allow him to gain health (and money) forever, as long as there is an enemy in the room.
An enemy spawned in a cleared room will also cause recharging to start.
Telekinesis: The item can be reactivated the moment the previous charge stops, making it always active.

collectibles.name: Dead Bird
desc_lines: Taking damage spawns a bird that attacks enemies
The bird deals 4.3 contact damage per second
Persists between rooms if player is at 1/2 hearts
collectibles.desc_raw: Taking damage spawns a bird that attacks enemies#The bird deals 4.3 contact damage per second#Persists between rooms if player is at 1/2 hearts

collectibles.name: Brimstone
desc_lines: ↓ x0.33 Fire rate multiplier
Isaac's tears are replaced by a chargeable blood beam
It deals 9x Isaac's damage over 0.63 seconds
collectibles.desc_raw: ↓ {{Tears}} x0.33 Fire rate multiplier#{{Chargeable}} Isaac's tears are replaced by a chargeable blood beam#{{Damage}} It deals 9x Isaac's damage over 0.63 seconds
synergies: 20/20 / The Inner Eye / Mutant Spider: Fires a spread of 2/3/4 Brimstone beams.

Despite the fire rate penalty caused by The Inner Eye and Mutant Spider, the overall DPS increase is more significant compared to 20/20. rHowever, if combined, 20/20 negates the fire rate penalty of the aforementioned items, which results in enormous damage output.

Almond Milk: Fires automatically upon reaching full charge. The beam lasts indefinitely as long as the fire key is held. The beam can change its direction while being fired, and can be aimed in any direction with joystick controls. The beam will also gain random worm trinket effects.
Increasing the tears stat will increase the damage and width of Brimstone as well.
Damage per Tick = (Damage × Fire Rate) ÷ 5
Azazel / Tainted Azazel: Picking up Brimstone with him will give him the regular version.

Apple! / Euthanasia / Tough Love: The beam can gain the damage multiplier from each of the items, although there is no visual difference. The bonus applies for the whole beam and not on a per-tick basis. Euthanasia loses its instant kill ability, however.
rThe bonus applies randomly on every tick of the beam, including the insta-kill ability from Euthanasia.

Anti-Gravity: An orb of energy expands, then shrinks while firing the beam after a delay. The orb still hurts enemies if they touch it. Isaac can start charging a new shot immediately after releasing the fire key without canceling the first shot, effectively allowing for a faster firing rate. It makes it harder to hit enemies with Azazel as the beam does not increase in range. rSize and damage of the beams no longer scales with fire rate, nor is it possible to sustain a perpetual beam at above 15 fire rate.
r Anti-Gravity + Soy Milk / Almond Milk: Allows auto-firing upon full charge, but without permanent beam uptime.
anr Anti-Gravity + Incubus: Isaac will fire Brimstone orbs, while Incubus will fire regular Brimstone beams.
r Both Isaac and Incubus fire Brimstone orbs.
Brain Worm: The beam will curve 90 degrees in order to attack opponents, effectively acting as a stronger form of homing. Very strong against multi-segment bosses such as Pin or Mother.
Brimstone
nr Additional copies of this item, such as from Diplopia / Crooked Penny, adds another beam, with no increase in charge time.
r The first additional copy of this item grants a 1.2× damage multiplier and +1 damage and doubles the beam's width. Further copies will only grant +1 damage.
If combined with Azazel's Birthright effect, the laser beam will be 4× wider.
The bigger beam granted by double Brimstone synergizes with other items as expected.

Chocolate Milk: Brimstone laser takes longer to fully charge, but can be released at any point. Width and damage of the laser increases with charge, from 25% damage and 50% width at minimum charge to 250% damage and 160% width at maximum charge.
Continuum: Brimstone laser travels through the wall and appears out the other side once.

Continuum + Rubber Cement: Instead of Continuum being overridden, the beam is reflected the second time it hits the wall. This results in it potentially overlapping with itself a total of three times.
Cricket's Body: Spawns split tears on the first enemy or obstacle the beam hits.
C Section: Shoots 4 fetus tears along with the Brimstone beam.
C Section + Soy Milk / Almond Milk: Small fetuses fire continuously alongside the beam.
C Section + The Ludovico Technique: Fetus tears no longer fire with the Brimstone circle, even on doing damage.

collectibles.name: Whore of Babylon
desc_lines: When on half a Red Heart or less:
↑ +0.3 Speed
↑ +1.5 Damage
collectibles.desc_raw: When on half a Red Heart or less:#↑ {{Speed}} +0.3 Speed#↑ {{Damage}} +1.5 Damage
synergies: Blood Rights / Razor Blade / IV Bag / Sumptorium: As these items takes Red Hearts before other heart types, it can be use to get the Whore of Babylon activation's condition easier.
Sharp Plug / Blood Bombs: Prioritize removing Red Hearts first although are conditional on having an applicable active item or having zero bombs.

Shard of Glass: Will slowly drain Isaac's red hearts upon taking damage, eventually triggering Whore of Babylon, unless Isaac has a Rotten Heart with no soul, black or bone hearts (unless playing as Eve).
Blood Oath: Consistently triggers Whore of Babylon at the start of each floor along with giving other bonuses.
Guppy's Paw / Potato Peeler: Can be used to remove red health containers to keep Whore of Babylon Active.
Magic Skin / VI - The Lovers?: Prioritize removing red heart containers with the downside of giving a Broken Heart with each use
Crow Heart: Can be used to remove red hearts while keeping soul and black hearts intact.
The Forgotten: By quickly switching to The Soul, Whore of Babylon can be triggered for the room if The Forgotten does not meet the trigger conditions.

Chooses the effect from a fixed list of items

desc_lines: +5 Bombs
Homing bombs
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Bomb}} +5 Bombs#Homing bombs
synergies: Dr. Fetus: The bombs that Isaac fires will be homing.

desc_lines: ↑ +1.2 Damage for the room
Deals 1 heart of damage to Isaac
After the first use in a room, deals half a heart instead
Removes Red Hearts first
collectibles.desc_raw: ↑ {{Damage}} +1.2 Damage for the room#{{Warning}} Deals 1 heart of damage to Isaac#After the first use in a room, deals half a heart instead#{{Heart}} Removes Red Hearts first
synergies: nr Invulnerability / Shield: The Razor can be used at no cost while invulnerability / shield is active, allowing damage to be significantly increased for the room.
nrIsaac's Heart: Isaac cannot take damage from the Razor Blade, allowing for infinite uses of the item.
Old Bandage / Shard of Glass: Isaac has a chance to drop a red heart while using the item.
Panic Button: Self-damage occurs before enemy damage, making it almost impossible to lose Angel or Devil chance.
nr The Polaroid: Isaac takes no damage while invincible, allowing for repeated free uses of the item.
The Wafer: Isaac loses half a heart instead of a full heart.
Whore of Babylon/Dark Prince's Crown: Can lower Isaac's health on demand to activate the bonus.

collectibles.name: Forget Me Now
desc_lines: Rerolls and restarts the entire floor
collectibles.desc_raw: Rerolls and restarts the entire floor
synergies: Glowing Hourglass + Schoolbag: Using Forget Me Now will fully recharge Glowing Hourglass on the new floor, using Glowing Hourglass after that will take Isaac back to the previous room while also giving back Forget Me Now. This allows for infinite usage of Glowing Hourglass as well as infinite retries of any room as long as Forget Me Now is used before the room is cleared.

desc_lines: Long range fly orbital

Sets your Speed to at least 1.5
Flight
Upon use, dashes in the direction of Isaac's movement
collectibles.desc_raw: While held:#{{Speed}} Sets your Speed to at least 1.5#Flight#Upon use, dashes in the direction of Isaac's movement
synergies: Mars: Using A Pony after dashing lowers the dash cooldown to approximately 1 second.

collectibles.name: Bomb Bag
desc_lines: Spawns 1 bomb pickup every 3 rooms
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Bomb}} Spawns 1 bomb pickup every 3 rooms
synergies: BFFS!: The familiar drops bombs in a repeating pattern of 2, 2 then 3 rooms.

collectibles.name: A Lump of Coal
desc_lines: Tears deal more damage the further they travel
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Damage}} Tears deal more damage the further they travel
synergies: Godhead
nr Tears' auras will increase in size and damage the farther they travel.
r Tears' auras will increase in size the farther they travel. The damage of the auras is unaffected.
Hook Worm: The movement pattern increases Coal's damage at the same effective distance.
The Ludovico Technique: Increases the tear's damage and size the farther the tear is from Isaac.
My Reflection: Hitting enemies with tears that initially missed will grant possibly considerable extra damage.
Pop!: Tears will increase their damage and size as they travel, and they are not limited by range.
Soy Milk: Coal's flat damage increase is not affected by Soy Milk's multiplier, so tears gain the full benefit from Coal.
Tiny Planet: The movement pattern allows much longer airtime and reasonably easy way to hit enemies with the powered-up tears.

collectibles.name: Guppy's Paw
desc_lines: Converts 1 heart container into 3 Soul Hearts
collectibles.desc_raw: {{SoulHeart}} Converts 1 heart container into 3 Soul Hearts
synergies: Broken Remote: The teleport will activate whenever Guppy's Paw is used, even if Isaac has no Red Heart Containers to trade. This can give any characters with no Red Hearts the ability to teleport any number of times for free.
Converter
nr Allows Isaac to attain up to 10 Red Heart Containers and 1 Soul Heart.
r Allows Isaac to attain up to 12 Red Heart Containers or any combination of Red Heart Containers and Soul Hearts.
+ Potato Peeler + Sharp Plug: Allows Isaac to generate infinite damage upgrades and Cube of Meat.
Tainted Eden: Soul Hearts granted by Guppy's Paw are not rerolled when Tainted Eden takes damage.
Dead Cat: In addition to contributing to the transformation, converting the Red Heart Container obtained after revival into three Soul Hearts effectively triples Isaac's HP.

collectibles.name: Guppy's Tail
desc_lines: 33% chance to replace the room clear reward with a chest
33% chance to spawn no room clear reward
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Chest}} 33% chance to replace the room clear reward with a chest#33% chance to spawn no room clear reward
synergies: Deep Pockets: If Guppy's Tail removes the room clear drop, it is turned into coins instead.
Guppy's Eye: Makes Key management easier by revealing which Locked Chests are worth opening and which are not.
The Left Hand: Turns all chests into Red Chests, allowing Isaac to get Red Chest items much more quickly. Useful when pursuing the Guppy transformation.
Mom's Key: Increases the number of drops from Chests and counteracts the increased chance to run into Locked Chests.
Options?: Chests may be accompanied by normal room drops or other chests of a type that's easier to open, mitigating the downsides.

Paper Clip: The Key scarcity problem is completely prevented, since Paper Clip causes Locked Chests to be openable without Keys, making Guppy's Tail's only downside the chance to replace room clears with nothing at all.
aIf Cain starts with the Trinket, entering Curse Rooms and opening Red Chests in hopes of finding Guppy's Tail early is a very effective strategy.

collectibles.name: IV Bag
desc_lines: Hurts Isaac for half a heart and spawns 1-2 coins
Removes Red Hearts first
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Coin}} Hurts Isaac for half a heart and spawns 1-2 coins#{{Heart}} Removes Red Hearts first
synergies: Isaac's Heart: Can be used without taking damage, resulting in an infinite amount of coins.
a Isaac still takes damage from this during The Guardian.
nr Increases each payout to 2-3 coins.
r Increases each payout to 2-3 coins on Normal mode and 2 on Hard mode.
Portable Slot: Can be alternated between to gain ridiculous amounts of pickups from only a few initial coins or hearts.
nr Scapular: Allows for infinite coins once Isaac is at half a red heart (or full red heart in Chapter 4 onward) by moving between rooms to reset Scapular's effect.
Keeper / Tainted Keeper: Spawns 0-1 coins instead.

collectibles.name: Best Friend
desc_lines: Spawns a decoy Isaac that attracts enemies and explodes after 5 seconds
collectibles.desc_raw: Spawns a decoy Isaac that attracts enemies and explodes after 5 seconds
synergies: Car Battery: Spawns two decoys on top of each other.
a+ Remote Detonator + Schoolbag: The decoy bomb is affected by remote detonator.

collectibles.name: Remote Detonator
desc_lines: +5 Bombs
Isaac's bombs no longer explode automatically
Upon use, detonates all of Isaac's bombs at once
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Bomb}} +5 Bombs#Isaac's bombs no longer explode automatically#Upon use, detonates all of Isaac's bombs at once
synergies: Anarchist Cookbook/Best Friend/Mr. Boom + Schoolbag: The active item bombs are affected by remote detonator.
Broken Remote: Allows unlimited teleportation.

Bob's Bladder: Bombs will continue to leave creep until they are manually detonated. Allows for maximization of creep damage, potentially dealing more damage over time to a boss than just a singular bomb.
Bobby-Bomb: Bombs placed will chase after the enemy, making it easier to do massive amounts of damage to enemies.
Curse of the Tower / XVI - The Tower: The Troll Bombs generated will not explode until the Remote Detonator is used, unlike normal Troll Bombs.
Dr. Fetus: Remote Detonator can be used to detonate the fired bombs early, which can be useful against moving enemies.
Number Two: The bombs automatically dropped by No. 2 will not detonate on their own, greatly reducing the risk of accidental explosive self-damage.
Rocket in a Jar: Rockets do not explode on impact with surfaces, requiring manual detonation.
Rocket in a Jar + Dr. Fetus: Rockets fired with Dr. Fetus will still explode on impact, but can be manually detonated.

Isaac can hold 2 trinkets

synergies: Tick: Lets you gain the benefits of the item without losing your only trinket slot.
a You cannot replace the Tick with The Match Stick once it is picked up, as it will only occupy the second trinket slot.

desc_lines: +5 Bombs
Isaac's bombs create a cloud of poison
Poison immunity
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Bomb}} +5 Bombs#{{Poison}} Isaac's bombs create a cloud of poison#{{Poison}} Poison immunity
synergies: Bob's Rotten Head: No effect.
r The explosion leaves behind a poison cloud.

Spawns a random penny trinket

synergies: Nuh Uh!: On the low chance this item spawns in Womb or Corpse, coins dropped will instead be replaced by drops from Nuh Uh!'s drop table.

Can only happen once per room
Exiting and re-entering the room allows the effect to trigger again
Doesn't trigger from health donations
collectibles.desc_raw: {{SoulHeart}} Isaac gains 1 Soul Heart when damaged down to half a heart#Can only happen once per room#Exiting and re-entering the room allows the effect to trigger again#{{Warning}} Doesn't trigger from health donations
synergies: Kamikaze!: Damage from Kamikaze's explosions will activate Scapular, allowing Isaac to trigger on-damage effects infinitely between rooms.

synergies: BFFS!: All future drops will happen every 3 coins. If the next scheduled drop at the moment of acquiring BFFS! requires more than 3 coins, the amount will not be lowered.
Mitre: Red Hearts that Bum drops can be converted into Soul Hearts.
Chaos: Bum Friend's item pedestals will be affected by Chaos, spawning an item from a random pool.

collectibles.name: Guppy's Head
desc_lines: Spawns 2-4 blue flies
collectibles.desc_raw: Spawns 2-4 blue flies
synergies: Car Battery: Increases range of flies spawned to 4-8.
Fish Tail: Doubles flies spawned.
9 Volt: Guppy's Head slowly gains charge while in a hostile room.

collectibles.name: Prayer Card
desc_lines: +1 Eternal Heart
collectibles.desc_raw: {{EternalHeart}} +1 Eternal Heart
synergies: Car Battery: Grants one full Heart Container upon use.
Tainted Eden: Health ups granted by eternal hearts are not rerolled when Tainted Eden takes damage.

collectibles.name: Notched Axe
desc_lines: Using the item makes Isaac hold the axe
Holding the axe allows Isaac to break rocks, secret room entrances and damage enemies
Landing a hit with the axe reduces its charge
Entering a new floor fully recharges the axe
collectibles.desc_raw: Using the item makes Isaac hold the axe#Holding the axe allows Isaac to break rocks, secret room entrances and damage enemies#Landing a hit with the axe reduces its charge#Entering a new floor fully recharges the axe
synergies: Anti-Gravity: Melee attacks will generate a hovering tear in the shape of the swing of the pickaxe.
The Battery: Causes remaining charge from the previous floor to carry over to the next one.
Bethany: Breaking Notched Axe and using a Soul Charge to activate it again will allow for a single use before immediately breaking.
Broken Padlock: Allows the Notched Axe to open locked chests and doors. The opening of chests does not use durability, however it will consume a key if Isaac has any.
Ipecac: Melee attacks will trigger an explosion when they makes contact with an enemy; unlike with the Forgotten's bone club, Isaac will take damage from these Ipecac explosions.
Iron Bar: Fully recharges the axe when picked up.
Technology: Melee attacks will cause a technology laser ring to appear around Isaac, damaging enemies.

collectibles.name: Infestation
desc_lines: Taking damage spawns 2-6 blue flies
collectibles.desc_raw: Taking damage spawns 2-6 blue flies

collectibles.name: Ipecac
desc_lines: ↑ +40 Damage
↓ x0.33 Fire rate multiplier
↓ x0.8 Range multiplier
↓ x0.8 Shot speed multiplier
Isaac's tears are fired in an arc
The tears explode and poison enemies where they land
collectibles.desc_raw: ↑ {{Damage}} +40 Damage#↓ {{Tears}} x0.33 Fire rate multiplier#↓ {{Range}} x0.8 Range multiplier#↓ {{Shotspeed}} x0.8 Shot speed multiplier#Isaac's tears are fired in an arc#{{Poison}} The tears explode and poison enemies where they land
synergies: Abyss: When Isaac uses Abyss to absorb Ipecac it turns into a larger slow moving green locust that deals x1.5 Isaac’s damage per tick and poisons enemies.

Brain Worm: The Ipecac tears will snap 90 degrees and rain down at nearby enemy should the shot miss, completely eliminating the shortcoming of having too much range and causing Ipecac tears to fly over enemy's head, missing them.
Brimstone / Azazel: No explosive beam or stat change. Beam poisons enemies.

a+nr Isaac shoots Ipecac tears while charging Brimstone. Both Brimstone and Ipecac shots modify Isaac's damage and tear delay stat, meaning that Ipecac fires slower and Brimstone charges slower, but Brimstone does an extra 40*13 damage. Take care to pre-charge Brimstone so that the Ipecac shot is on cooldown as Isaac enters a room.

r Blood beam now does poison damage and will explode on connection with an enemy which can damage the character if close enough. The character doesn't gain the Damage Up or Tears Down effects. The flat damage bonus is reduced to +2, but Isaac's tear rate stat is not modified. The beam explodes on the first enemy or obstacle it hits every other tick, dealing 10 + twice of Isaac's Damage. The explosions do not occur when the beam narrows, meaning that they occur only twice per shot without Soy Milk / Almond Milk. The beam and its explosions poison enemies.
Cricket's Body: The main tear splits into four explosive splash tears. The splash tears can still damage Isaac.
r Ipecac's x0.8 range multiplier does not stack with Cricket's Body's own x0.8 range multiplier.
C Section: The fetuses explode once they touch an enemy, poisoning and dealing damage equal to Isaac's Damage.
Dr. Fetus: Bombs explode on contact, do no damage to Isaac and become Bob's Curse bombs.
nr The damage is massively increased, as the +40 damage from Ipecac is run through the damage multiplier of Dr. Fetus.
r The flat damage bonus is reduced to +2. Bombs leave a poison cloud that damages enemies. With Jacob and Esau only the character with the items is immune to the explosions.
Dr. Fetus + Monstro's Lung: Renders Isaac no longer immune to his shots. r The flat damage bonus is increased back to +40.
Dr. Fetus + Sad Bombs: Isaac is not immune to the explosive shots created by the Sad Bombs' effect.
Epic Fetus: Missiles turn green and are poisonous. Damage greatly increases as the flat damage bonus stacks with Epic Fetus' explosive multiplier.
Flat Stone: The Ipecac shots cause full-damage explosions instead of lesser splash damage when they bounce off the floor, though they can also harm Isaac.
r Each bounce creates an explosion that deals half Isaac's damage as well as dealing the regular splash damage. The bounce explosion radius is slightly larger than the splash radius.

The Forgotten: The flat damage bonus is reduced to +4. Melee attacks become poisonous and explosive; he cannot be hurt by the blasts but is pushed back by them. The explosions deal 1.5x damage. The charged throwing attack is only poisonous. The Soul uses Ipecac as normal.

r The flat damage bonus is reduced to +2. The thrown bone club can explode once on enemy contact while being sent out, but not while returning. These explosions deal from 10 + 2.8x to 10 + 6x Damage based on how much the attack is charged, and do no self-damage.
r When the skeleton form has Technology, the thrown bone club constantly explodes and deals damage to The Forgotten.
Haemolacria: Tear delay is increased by 1/3 compared to either of the items on their own, but Isaac now fires Ipecac shots that release poisonous, high-damage but non-explosive split tears on impact.
r Ipecac's x0.8 range multiplier does not stack with Haemolacria's own x0.8 range multiplier.
The Ludovico Technique: Tear damage is increased by 4, explodes when killing an enemy, and comes back to Isaac.
a+nr +4 flat damage bonus, no explosion.
r +2 flat damage bonus, no explosion.

Maw Of The Void: The ring becomes poisonous and explodes on the first enemy or obstacle contact it hits every other tick, dealing 10 + twice of Isaac's Damage. The explosions do not occur for the first four ticks.

collectibles.name: Tough Love
desc_lines: 10% chance to shoot teeth that deal 3.2x Isaac's damage
100% chance at 9 luck
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Damage}} 10% chance to shoot teeth that deal 3.2x Isaac's damage#{{Luck}} 100% chance at 9 luck

collectibles.name: The Mulligan
desc_lines: Tears have a 1/6 chance to spawn a blue fly when hitting an enemy
collectibles.desc_raw: Tears have a 1/6 chance to spawn a blue fly when hitting an enemy
synergies: Azazel/Brimstone: Rapid damage ticks can cause large amounts of blue flies to spawn.
Chocolate Milk: The Mulligan's ability isn't affected by the charge time, meaning rapidly firing uncharged shots will generate a ton of blue flies.
Cricket's Body / The Parasite: Has a 16.67% chance to spawn a fly on every hit by a split shot, which boosts the spawning of flies immensely.
Dr. Fetus: Bombs have a chance to spawn blue flies when damaging enemies.
Finger!: Rapidly spawns blue flies when pointed at an enemy.
Urn of Souls: Fires have a chance to spawn blue flies.

collectibles.name: Technology 2
desc_lines: ↓ x0.67 Fire rate multiplier
Replaces Isaac's right eye tears with a continuous laser
The laser deals 2x Isaac's damage per second
collectibles.desc_raw: ↓ {{Tears}} x0.67 Fire rate multiplier#Replaces Isaac's right eye tears with a continuous laser#{{Damage}} The laser deals 2x Isaac's damage per second
synergies: 8 Inch Nails / Blister / Pisces: The laser will consistently push back enemies.
Blood Clot / Chemical Peel: Since Isaac's right eye is replaced by Technology 2, he can only fire tears from his left eye, which gives all his tears higher damage and range.
Brimstone: Laser fires continuously while Brimstone charges at normal rate.
Chocolate Milk: The laser fires while Chocolate Milk charges at normal rate.
Cricket's Body : The laser will rapidly split into small low damage tears upon impact with an enemy or obstacle.
Cursed Eye: The laser fires while Cursed Eye charges at normal rate, retaining the teleporting effect.
r Tears are replaced with lasers, fired from the same eye of Technology 2.
Epic Fetus: The laser fires while the missiles are being aimed.
Euthanasia / Explosivo / Holy Light / Little Horn / Nose Goblin / Sinus Infection: Every damage tick from the laser can also apply these tear effects and does so rapidly.
Explosivo’s 60 point explosion damage bonus is unaffected by the laser’s reduced damage making the combination incredibly effective.
Incubus / Lil Clot / Sumptorium / Twisted Pair: The familiars fire a continuous laser.
Ipecac: Laser damage increases dramatically while also gaining the poison effect.

Lachryphagy: Significantly shortens the range of the laser. A piercing Lachryphagy tear spawns at the end of the laser every tick, rapidly feeding off of each other and bursting when standing still. This is an extremely powerful combo that alone can provide enough damage output to challenge any boss in the game.
A fire rate reduction such as from The Inner Eye or a few Tears Down pills makes the combo even stronger, as tears will grow even larger before bursting.
Lead Pencil: Replaces tears with lasers similar to Technology. These are shot from the eye that shoots Technology 2, doing damage alongside the Technology 2 beam.
Loki's Horns: Additional tears are affected by Technology 2 but are replaced by Technology's lasers, dealing 100% of Isaac's damage and with 16 luck gives you Technology 2's fire rate.
Lost Contact: The laser destroys all hostile projectiles that come into contact with it.
The Ludovico Technique: Isaac simultaneously controls a tear and fires the laser; however, Isaac will only fire the Technology 2 laser in the direction that the tear is currently facing.
r The Ludovico Technique also makes the laser spectral.
A Lump of Coal: Damage increases the further away the enemy is, on a multiplier that is roughly +20% per tile of distance.
Marked: Isaac simultaneously controls a big red target on the ground and fires the laser in the cardinal directions.
r The laser now continuously fires towards the crosshair along with tears.
Mom's Knife: The laser fires while the Knife charges at normal rate.
Monstro's Lung: The laser fires while Monstro's Lung charges at normal rate. No reduction in tear burst size.
The Parasite: Laser splits into two perpendicular beams upon hitting an obstacle.

collectibles.name: Mutant Spider
desc_lines: ↓ x0.42 Fire rate multiplier
Isaac shoots 4 tears at once
collectibles.desc_raw: ↓ {{Tears}} x0.42 Fire rate multiplier#Isaac shoots 4 tears at once
synergies: a+nr 20/20: Shoots 6 tears.

The Ludovico Technique: The tear flashes between normal and the higher-damaging Chemical Peel tear.
Mom's Knife: Each tick of the Knife's damage has a high chance to gain the bonus, with no visual difference.

9 Volt + Yum Heart: Isaac could potentially become invincible on early floors or with The Wafer, losing only half a heart per three hits taken.
The Battery + r Sharp Plug: Isaac gains one charge from Habit for every half-heart of damage that Sharp Plug deals, effectively granting double the charges when using Sharp Plug.
Dull Razor + Schoolbag: Using the Dull Razor activates the Habit, but this does not restore its charge, only that of the item in the Schoolbag.

Dull Razor + Car Battery + The Battery: Using Dull Razor activates the Habit, fully recharging Dull Razor, allowing for infinite uses of the Dull Razor, infinite invincibility frames, and infinite proccing of on-hit effects.
Larynx: Both on-hit charge effects stack, granting Larynx 2 charges per hit.

collectibles.name: Epic Fetus
desc_lines: Instead of shooting tears, aim a crosshair
A rocket lands on the crosshair after 1.5 seconds
Rockets deal 20x Isaac's damage
collectibles.desc_raw: Instead of shooting tears, aim a crosshair#A rocket lands on the crosshair after 1.5 seconds#{{Damage}} Rockets deal 20x Isaac's damage
synergies: 20/20/Conjoined/Bookworm/Mutant Spider/The Inner Eye: Fires a barrage of missiles corresponding with the amount of tears.

Bomber Boy: Missiles explode in a large cross-shaped blast, with each explosion dealing 0.75x times damage. Unlike with regular bombs, getting hit by every separate explosion from Epic Fetus won't decrease the damage for each successive explosion, drastically increasing damage.
Blood Bombs: Every missile explosion leaves a large, lasting pool of red creep.
Blood Clot/Chemical Peel: Each missile has a 50% chance to deal 20 or 40 points more damage, respectively.
Poison tears: This is guaranteed by Bob's Curse, Ipecac, and Scorpio, and also has a chance to trigger from The Common Cold, Pinky Eye or Serpent's Kiss.
r Explosions leave behind a cloud of poison gas that will poison enemies in contact with it aside from the main target.
Brimstone: Missile explosions shoot ten blood lasers in all directions.
Brimstone Bombs: Missile explosions have a chance to shoot four Brimstone lasers in the cardinal directions. The chance scales with Isaac's luck.
Butt Bombs: Missile explosions will cloud up the screen, briefly confusing and dealing 10 damage to all enemies in the room.
Eye Sore: More marks appear orbiting around where you're aiming.
Fire Mind/Hot Bombs: Missile explosions leave behind a fire that damage enemies on contact. Fire Mind's burn effect is overridden.

a+ The Forgotten: The charge attack is replaced by a missile in the Forgotten's hand and a crosshair that may be moved for an indefinite amount of time. The missile is thrown at the crosshair when all attack buttons are released, with an additional melee swing in the same direction. The melee attack is unaffected.
Glitter Bombs: Missile explosions have a small chance of spawning pickups.
a Homing tears: The crosshair slowly slides towards enemies.
Host Hat/Pyromaniac: Isaac becomes immune to his own airstrikes.
Incubus: Incubus fires a missile alongside Isaac's.
nrIpecac: Missiles have a poison effect, have a larger blast radius and deal drastically increased damage, as the +40 damage from Ipecac is run through the damage multiplier of Epic Fetus.
r Adds flat +2 damage instead of +40. Explosions leave behind a cloud of poison gas that will poison enemies in contact with it aside from the main target.
Lil Clot / Tainted Eve: The clots have their own targeting reticles and fire simultaneously with Isaac.
Mom's Knife: Missile explosions shoot a ring of ten knives.
With a second Mom's Knife a second missile will be shot similar to 20/20.
Monstro's Lung: 2 to 4 additional crosshairs orbit the main crosshair, and missiles are fired onto each crosshair.
Mysterious Liquid: Every missile explosion leaves a small, lasting pool of creep.
Nancy Bombs: Explosions have a chance to be affected by any of the bomb-modifier effects that synergize with Epic Fetus.
Polyphemus: Increased tear delay, but massively increases the missiles' blast radius and damage, as Polyphemus' damage multiplier is run through the damage multiplier of Epic Fetus.

synergies: 20/20: Damage is reduced and tear rate is increased.

Abyss: When Polyphemus is picked up instead of being converted into a locust, it grants all of Abyss's locusts the high damage up without suffering the tears down allowing Isaac to increase his damage rate substantially the more items he uses Abyss on.
Dr. Fetus: Increases tear delay, and after the first bomb explodes, a second bomb appears where the first one exploded, doing roughly half the damage of the first.
The Inner Eye/Mutant Spider/C Section: The tear rate is not lowered further, but the effect on damage becomes a flat +5 bonus instead.
The Forgotten: The Forgotten's bone club becomes massive. It deals very high damage but has a very low swing rate.
Rock Bottom: Allows Isaac to keep his previous fire rate in addition to the massive damage boost.
Shard of Glass: The rapidly spawning red tears do full damage.

Box of Friends: Spawns an additional foot for the rest of the room.
Extension Cord: Bolts of electricity will arc between the shadow or the foot and Isaac and his familiars.

Gnawed Leaf: Allows Isaac to stay invincible while Daddy Longlegs clears the room, granting Isaac a slow but effective way of completely avoiding damage. This combination is especially useful as The Lost and Tainted Lost.
Spin to Win: Boosts the foot's damage while spinning.

synergies: A Card Against Humanity: Spawned Poops have a great chance to turn into Golden Poops. 10% at 0 luck and 100% at 18 luck.
Deep Pockets: Increases maximum contact damage. The damage caps at 203.3 damage with 999 coins.
E. Coli: Enemies touched are turned into Golden Poop.

nr Epic Fetus + E. Coli: Launching a rocket at Isaac will turn the rocket into a golden poop, causing the explosion to break it automatically and spawning coins, which allows health to be exchanged for money.
Flush!: Touching poops made from enemies turns them into Golden Poops just before being destroyed dropping the normal Golden Poop pickups including Counterfeit Penny.
The Poop: Using The Poop has a high chance to spawn Golden Poop.
nr 50% at 0 luck and 100% at 5 luck.
r 33% at 0 luck and 100% at 7 luck.
Re-Lax: Spawned Poops have a chance to turn into Golden Poops. 25% at 0 luck and 100% at 8 luck.

VII - The Chariot/VII - The Chariot?

Tooth and Nail

collectibles.name: Humbleing Bundle
desc_lines: Pickups have a 50% chance to be doubled
collectibles.desc_raw: Pickups have a 50% chance to be doubled
synergies: Contract From Below: Single pickup drops have a 2/3 chance to be doubled, which then become their doubled versions by Humbling Bundle.
This results in an average increase from 1.33x more pickups to 2.67x more.

IV Bag + Old Bandage + Humbleing Bundle / Maggy's Bow: The damage from IV Bag has a chance to spawn 2 hearts, which can be picked up to create further coins and possibly even heal hearts with enough Luck.
r Humbleing Bundle only has a 50% chance to double the hearts dropped, but Old Bandage may still drop 1 heart.
The Jar: Allows for replicating hearts. Using the jar with 4 hearts inside it will cause 8 to drop, allowing Isaac to create an infinite amount of Red Hearts.

collectibles.name: Fanny Pack
desc_lines: Taking damage has a 50% chance to spawn a random pickup
collectibles.desc_raw: Taking damage has a 50% chance to spawn a random pickup
synergies: Fanny Pack: Having multiples of Fanny Pack neither increases the amount of drops, nor the drop chance.
Blood Oath: Each half-heart lost has a chance of spawning a pickup from the fanny pack.
Scapular: Allows for infinite pickups once Isaac is at half a red heart (or full red heart in Chapter 4 onward) by moving between rooms to reset Scapular's effect.
Placenta: Similar to Scapular, allows for unlimited pickups provided a disregard for time.
Sharp Plug: Each half a heart lost from the use of sharp plug has a chance of spawning a picking from the fanny pack.

Spindown Dice + Greed Mode: Turns into Humbleing Bundle when rerolled by Spindown Dice (which is not normally obtainable in Greed Mode) and this allows penny drops from each wave a chance of turning into double pennies.

collectibles.name: Sharp Plug
desc_lines: Using an uncharged active item fully recharges it at the cost of half a heart per missing charge
Removes Red Hearts first
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Battery}} Using an uncharged active item fully recharges it at the cost of half a heart per missing charge#{{Heart}} Removes Red Hearts first
synergies: r Damage-triggered items: Each half heart used up by activating Sharp Plug triggers damage effects separately, allowing multiple payouts.
Habit: Each damage taken triggers the extra charge, which can be seen when Isaac has The Battery, or when using Sharp Plug without having enough health to fully charge the active.
Glowing Hourglass: As long as Isaac has more than 2 full hearts, time can be rewinded indefinitely since the health used to recharge the item is refunded upon use.
Jacob's Ladder / 120 Volt: Electricity deals 75% of Isaac's tear damage if he owns 1 of these items, to 100% if Isaac owns both.
Keeper: The Keeper takes only one heart of damage.
r Expends health for charges at the rate of 2 charges per 1 Coin Heart. If an item is 1-charge, removes 1 Coin Heart.
Schoolbag: The active item in the main slot is fully charged as normal and the secondary slot gains as many charges as the main one did.
r: Only the front item is charged.

Tainted Magdalene: Tainted Magdalene's Yum Heart can be used infinitely, allowing for infinite invincibility frames from taking damage. Paired with Tainted Magdalene's melee attack, this can easily win a run.
r+ Bleeding hearts grant 1 charge per full bleeding heart. Non bleeding hearts grant charges as normal.
The Wafer: Reduces the health cost to half a heart (except for Keeper).

Big Fan: Slows down the head orbital.
Diplopia: Additional copies provide the stat increase as well as an orbital head familiar that doesn't fire.

desc_lines: Lv1: Orbital
Lv2: Orbital that shoots charmed tears
Lv3: Bandage Girl
Lv4: Super Bandage Girl
collectibles.desc_raw: Lv1: Orbital#{{Charm}} Lv2: Orbital that shoots charmed tears#{{Charm}} Lv3: Bandage Girl#{{Charm}} Lv4: Super Bandage Girl
synergies: Book of Revelations: Ensures that Harbingers that haven't already been defeated will replace their respective floor's boss, making it drastically easier to collect multiple Balls of Bandages.
Box of Friends / Monster Manual: Additional copies of Ball of Bandages combine with the first, increasing its level as normal.
Potato Peeler: Spawns a Ball of Bandages instead of a Cube of Meat. If Isaac already has a Cube of Meat, that is still spawned.

collectibles.name: Champion Belt
desc_lines: ↑ +1 Damage
Champion enemy chance goes from 5% to 20%
Doesn't increase chance of champion bosses
collectibles.desc_raw: ↑ {{Damage}} +1 Damage#Champion enemy chance goes from 5% to 20%#Doesn't increase chance of champion bosses

collectibles.name: Butt Bombs
desc_lines: +5 Bombs
Explosions concuss and damage every enemy in the room
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Bomb}} +5 Bombs#{{Confusion}} Explosions concuss and damage every enemy in the room

collectibles.name: Gnawed Leaf
desc_lines: After 1 second of inactivity, Isaac becomes invincible
collectibles.desc_raw: After 1 second of inactivity, Isaac becomes invincible
synergies: 7 Seals: One of the more versatile familiars that works with Gnawed Leaf. The varied forms and attacks of this familiar make it capable of clearing pretty much any room given time. The locusts it produces often fly to Isaac which takes care of chasing enemies, but its damaging creep and chasing attacks can reach even the farthest enemies.
120 Volt: With enough damage, 120 Volt's zaps becomes far more efficient at killing than orbitals.
At 15 damage minimum, it surpasses most orbitals.
At 30 damage or more, it surpasses Sacrificial Dagger.

Angry Fly: Aggressively attacks enemies even if they're far away, but can end up harmlessly orbiting slow or stationary enemies indefinitely, or trying to attack invulnerable enemies. It does better with slow or stationary bosses than regular enemies, diving in to attack periodically instead of harmlessly orbiting.

Daddy Longlegs: Allows Isaac to easily clear most rooms or bosses while in statue form. Daddy Longlegs cannot damage bosses like The Shell and won't target enemies that are a long distance from Isaac. In a pinch, Mom's Toenail works the same, albeit much slower.
Damocles: If Isaac is in statue form when the sword falls, he will survive.
Dark Arts: While Isaac is surrounded by enemies, he will not take damage and will safely go into the shadow state. (Even if enemies are in pointblank range.)
Decap Attack: The head can be used on stationary bosses to wither them down.
E. Coli /Midas' Touch /Serpent's Kiss / The Virus: Monsters touching the statue will be affected without Isaac taking collision damage.
Empty Vessel: if Empty Vessel's requirement is fulfilled, Isaac can safely wait for the shield to activate, allowing to deal damage without leaving invulnerability.
IBS: Orbitals and other things (such as 120 Volt) can trigger IBS while in statue form, however, Isaac will still be invincible. This can be really good if a Stinky Poop is held above his head.
Kamikaze!: Statue form will break, but the initial explosion will not damage Isaac, therefore granting Isaac quasi-unlimited bombs.
The Ludovico Technique: The hovering tear will still deal damage and the statue form won't break as long as the attack buttons aren't being pressed.
The Ludovico Technique + Strange Attractor: Can pull enemies in while remaining a statue, clearing out rooms easily.
Mom's Knife: The knife can damage enemies while Isaac is a statue.
rMom's Heels: While invincible, Isaac can still deal contact damage.
Monstrance: The aura makes quick work of chasing enemies.
The Pinking Shears: Isaac's body will attack enemies while his head is invincible.
Placenta: Isaac can safely regenerate Red Hearts in any room, making it easy to have enough health from any mistake at the cost of time.
Robo-Baby 2.0: Since it fires automatically, this familiar is one of the more effective synergies, since it will even shoot off screen as it’s knocked around.

Salvation: The aura can trigger from nearby enemies while Isaac is safely a statue, and the damaging beams when this happens can reach farther enemies. This powerful attack can make quick work of bosses that chase Isaac.
Spin to Win: Gnawed Leaf will activate while using Spin to Win, as long as Isaac is stationary. This allows for a brief moment of high damage against enemies that chase Isaac.

Sprinkler: Can be placed upon entering a room, and will continue to fire when Isaac is invincible, meaning any room can be cleared without the risk of Isaac taking damage. This theoretically allows end-game bosses to be beaten without having to move.
Super Magnet: Pulls enemies into Isaac, can be extremely powerful if combined with orbitals that allow contact damage.

collectibles.name: Spiderbaby
desc_lines: Taking damage spawns 3-5 blue spiders
collectibles.desc_raw: Taking damage spawns 3-5 blue spiders

collectibles.name: Guppy's Collar
desc_lines: 50% chance to revive with half a heart on death
collectibles.desc_raw: 50% chance to revive with half a heart on death

collectibles.name: Lost Contact
desc_lines: ↓ -0.15 Shot speed
Isaac's tears destroy enemy shots
collectibles.desc_raw: ↓ {{Shotspeed}} -0.15 Shot speed#Isaac's tears destroy enemy shots
synergies: Anti-Gravity: Placing many tears around Isaac creates a safe zone, especially with larger tears.

Brimstone/Maw Of The Void/Technology/Technology 2: Beam/ring/laser destroys projectiles, although only during its damage tick interval. The projectiles are not instantly destroyed when they touch the beam/ring/laser.
Compound Fracture/Cricket's Body/The Parasite: Split tears will be shielded. The Parasite's split tears will not appear to have shields.
Eye of Belial: Piercing through a projectile will trigger Eye of Belial's double damage and homing effect. Belial tears will still block shots.

rThe Forgotten: Enemy shots may be reflected with melee attacks. The damage of the reflected shots depends on their size and properties, with standard enemy shots dealing 6 damage, champion and homing shots dealing 7 and explosive ones dealing 50.
Jacob's Ladder: Destroyed tears via shot-blocking will trigger the electric arc effect.
The Ludovico Technique: Can block an infinite amount of enemy shots if the tear is placed on top of a monster. The projectiles that they attempt to shoot will be immediately destroyed.
nrMom's Knife: The knife will destroy projectiles in its path without being affected.
rThe knife does not destroy projectiles.
Piercing tears: The tears can pass through multiple enemy projectiles. This means that one tear can hurt the enemy and block its projectiles.
Polyphemus: Enemy shots are treated like enemies with 10 health, which are deducted from the Polyphemus tear. If it has strength remaining afterward, it will continue.
Sinus Infection/Mucormycosis: Tears that stick to enemies will block shots, and will not be destroyed in the process.
Soy Milk: The constant stream of tears will cut through any complex attack patterns with ease.
Spirit Sword: Sword swings reflect projectiles.

Almond Milk: The random tear effects can allow more shots to be blocked, as effects such as Ouroboros Worm, My Reflection, and Tiny Planet can cause tears to wander outside of the main stream of tears. However, this, in combination with the lower fire rate of Almond Milk compared to Soy Milk, makes Almond Milk slightly less effective than Soy Milk at blocking shots in the direction Isaac is shooting.
Tech X: The rings block enemy shots.
r The rings sometimes block enemy shots. [more testing/debugging required]
Trisagion: Beams will block shots and continue on.

collectibles.name: Anemic
desc_lines: ↑ +1.5 Range
When taking damage Isaac leaves a trail of blood creep for the room
The creep deals 6 damage per second
collectibles.desc_raw: ↑ {{Range}} +1.5 Range#{{Timer}} When taking damage Isaac leaves a trail of blood creep for the room#The creep deals 6 damage per second
