desc_lines: Orbital
Deals 105 contact damage per second
Blocks and attracts enemy shots
Blocking 10 shots in one room spawns an Eternal Heart
collectibles.desc_raw: Orbital#Deals 105 contact damage per second#Blocks and attracts enemy shots#{{EternalHeart}} Blocking 10 shots in one room spawns an Eternal Heart
synergies: nrFriendly enemies: Spawning a friendly enemy that shoots bullets allows Eternal Hearts to be farmed since the friendly enemy will fire at Isaac if no hostile enemies are in the room.
Sacrificial Altar: This familiar will also reward 2 Black Hearts when sacrificed.

collectibles.name: Friend Zone
desc_lines: Midrange fly orbital
Deals 45 contact damage per second
collectibles.desc_raw: Midrange fly orbital#Deals 45 contact damage per second

collectibles.name: Lost Fly
desc_lines: Moves along walls/obstacles in the room
Deals 30 contact damage per second
Nearby enemies target the fly
collectibles.desc_raw: Moves along walls/obstacles in the room#Deals 30 contact damage per second#Nearby enemies target the fly

collectibles.name: Scatter Bombs
desc_lines: +5 Bombs
Isaac's bombs scatter into 4-5 tiny bombs
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Bomb}} +5 Bombs#Isaac's bombs scatter into 4-5 tiny bombs

collectibles.name: Sticky Bombs
desc_lines: +5 Bombs
Isaac's bombs stick to enemies and leave white slowing creep
Killing an enemy with a bomb spawns blue spiders
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Bomb}} +5 Bombs#{{Slow}} Isaac's bombs stick to enemies and leave white slowing creep#Killing an enemy with a bomb spawns blue spiders
synergies: Bob's Rotten Head: sticks to an enemy, the explosion is delayed but will deal 110 damage.
r No longer sticks to enemies.
Bomber Boy: Greatly increases the size of the creep puddle.

collectibles.name: Epiphora
desc_lines: ↑ Shooting in one direction gradually increases fire rate up to 200% and decreases accuracy
collectibles.desc_raw: ↑ {{Tears}} Shooting in one direction gradually increases fire rate up to 200% and decreases accuracy
synergies: Analog Stick: Epiphora's bonus will not reset when switching fire diagonally, as long as Isaac's head doesn't turn.
Continuum: Reduces the need to change firing direction, allowing Isaac to maintain the increased rate of fire.
Cursed Eye: Epiphora's tear rate multiplier affects the number of tears in a burst for a total of ten shots with a full Epiphora charge.
Dr. Fetus / Ipecac: Firing bombs continually will decrease tear delay, although doing so is not recommended since it increases the risk of self-damage.
The Forgotten: When tapping the attack button in a single direction repeatedly, the Ephiphora effect will cause the bone swings to speed up.
How to Jump: Changing firing direction while jumping does not reset the bonus, similar to when changing rooms.
Incubus: Incubus's fire rate will match Isaac's.
The Ludovico Technique: Fire rate increases while moving the tear in one direction; it has a negligible effect during normal gameplay.
Marked: Both items work as normal, with tear rate gradually increasing until Isaac turns his head, which can happen via him or the crosshair moving too out of parallel.
Rubber Cement: Reduces the need to change firing direction allowing Isaac to maintain the increased rate of fire.

Rock Bottom: Isaac will never lose the benefits of Epiphora, even after he stops firing. This effectively makes Epiphora a substantial tears up. Fully charging Ephiphora is still needed to gain a higher tears stat if an item that would increase the tears stat is picked up.
Tractor Beam: Fire rate still increases when shooting in one direction, but will stay on the line (i.e. no loss in accuracy).
Loki's Horns / Mom's Eye: Allows for firing in multiple directions at once, decreasing need to change firing direction.

collectibles.name: Continuum
desc_lines: ↑ +3 Range
Spectral tears
Tears can travel through one side of the screen and come out the other side
collectibles.desc_raw: ↑ {{Range}} +3 Range#Spectral tears#Tears can travel through one side of the screen and come out the other side
synergies: rAzazel / Brimstone / Revelation / Tainted Azazel / Technology: The laser continues behind Isaac and back once again, potentially hitting enemies twice in one blast.
Bob's Rotten Head: Bob's Rotten Head can travel through walls and appear out of the opposite wall.
r Does not travel through the wall; instead bounces off it like with Rubber Cement.
a+ The Forgotten: The skeleton form's charge attack can loop through walls.
A Lump of Coal: Tears will continually gain damage when passing through walls.
Mom's Knife: Causes Mom's Knife to appear on the opposite wall if thrown through one. It then returns through the wall to Isaac.
Mom's Knife + Brimstone: The barrage of knives flies across the room until it hits the wall twice.
My Reflection: When shooting through a wall, tears speed up towards Isaac rather than returning through the original wall. With enough shot speed and range the tears can loop twice.
Tech X: Laser rings will go through walls, extra distance depending on shot speed.
nr Tech X + Incubus: Incubus's laser rings cannot go through walls. This applies to Lilith too.
r Tech X + Incubus: Laser rings travel through walls and continue on the other side of the room.
Tractor Beam: Causes the tears to loop right back in front of Isaac after they go offscreen.

collectibles.name: Mr. Dolly
desc_lines: ↑ +0.7 Tears
↑ +2.5 Range
Spawns 3 random hearts
collectibles.desc_raw: ↑ {{Tears}} +0.7 Tears#↑ {{Range}} +2.5 Range#{{UnknownHeart}} Spawns 3 random hearts

collectibles.name: Curse of the Tower
desc_lines: Taking damage spawns 6 Troll Bombs
The Troll Bombs inherit Isaac's bomb effects
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Warning}} Taking damage spawns 6 Troll Bombs#The Troll Bombs inherit Isaac's bomb effects
synergies: Blasting Cap: Spawned Troll Bombs can drop extra bomb pickups.
Glitter Bombs: Each troll bomb has a chance to spawn a pickup or a trinket including hearts for repeated use.
The Polaroid: If Isaac is damaged when he has half a Red Heart or less, the shield will activate and protect him from the Troll Bombs.
Pyromaniac: Troll Bombs will heal Isaac instead, allowing for essentially infinite health with proper usage.
r Due to Pyromaniac being weakened to healing half a heart per explosion in Repentance, this synergy requires The Wafer to continue being effective past Chapter 3/Chapter 3.5.
Remote Detonator: The Troll Bombs generated will not explode until Remote Detonator is activated, unlike regular Troll Bombs.
Safety Scissors: The Troll Bombs generated will be converted into bomb pickups rather than exploding.

collectibles.name: Charged Baby
desc_lines: Every 30 seconds while in an uncleared room, the familiar can:
Spawn a Battery (max 2 per room)
Add one charge to the active item (max 2 per room)
Petrify all enemies in the room
collectibles.desc_raw: Every 30 seconds while in an uncleared room, the familiar can:#{{Battery}} Spawn a Battery (max 2 per room)#{{Battery}} Add one charge to the active item (max 2 per room)#{{Petrify}} Petrify all enemies in the room
synergies: BFFS!: Familiar is bigger and drops Lil' Batteries more often. No effect on its other abilities.

collectibles.name: Dead Eye
desc_lines: ↑ Consecutive tear hits on enemies grant +25% damage (max +100%)
Missing has a chance to reset the multiplier
collectibles.desc_raw: ↑ {{Damage}} Consecutive tear hits on enemies grant +25% damage (max +100%)#Missing has a chance to reset the multiplier
synergies: Compound Fracture/Haemolacria/Lachryphagy: Main tears and burst tears will contribute to damage multiplier.
Lachryphagy Firing into the floating tear won't reset the multiplier unless it hits a wall. The burst tears never reset the multiplier.
Dark Arts: Hitting enemies/bullets successfully with Dark Arts contributes to Dead Eye's damage multiplier.
The Ludovico Technique: Damage bonus increases until it hits the max of 2x. Since the controllable tear never hits the ground or wall, the bonus cannot be lost.
Petrification/Slow items: Enemies will be petrified or slowed, making them easier to hit without missing.
Pop!: Tears that miss an enemy and do not hit a wall or obstacle will not fall to the ground immediately, allowing many damage-amplified tears to be fired before the damage boost is lost.

Proptosis: Tears are larger and easier to hit with at a low range. Additionally, if the tear fades out of existence instead of running out of range or hitting a wall/obstacle, it does not count as a miss.
Spirit Sword: Dead Eye will increase the damage every slash until it hits the max of 2x. The bonus can be lost if a sword projectile doesn't hit anything.
Tiny Planet: Isaac's tears no longer hit walls and stay in the air for longer, allowing many damage-amplified tears to be fired before the damage boost is lost.
Akeldama: The tears in the trail will contribute to the damage boost, making it possible to stack damage while having items that do not interact with Dead Eye on their own (such as Brimstone).

collectibles.name: Holy Light
desc_lines: 10% chance to shoot holy tears, which spawn a beam of light on hit
50% chance at 9 luck
The beams deals 3x Isaac's damage
collectibles.desc_raw: 10% chance to shoot holy tears, which spawn a beam of light on hit#{{Luck}} 50% chance at 9 luck#{{Damage}} The beams deals 3x Isaac's damage
synergies: Abyss: When Isaac uses Abyss to absorb Holy Light it turns into a glowing cyan locust. Every tick of damage it deals has a chance to call down a beam of light dealing triple Isaac’s damage.
Bob's Rotten Head: Has a chance to throw a Holy Light head that spawns a damaging beam of light dealing (nr4×) r3× the explosion damage when it hits an enemy.
Compound Fracture / Cricket's Body / The Parasite: Holy Light tears will have split tears with Holy Light effect; normal tears will not have split tears with Holy Light effect.

Lachryphagy: If Holy Light tears feed normal tears, fed normal tears will not gain Holy Light effect; however if normal tears feed Holy Light tears, it will just increase the size of fed Holy Light tears.
The Ludovico Technique: The controlled tear will sometimes glow and trigger the beam.
C Section: Glowing shots will rapidly strike light beams on contact, making short work of most enemies.

collectibles.name: Host Hat
desc_lines: Grants immunity to explosions and falling projectiles
25% chance to reflect enemy shots
collectibles.desc_raw: Grants immunity to explosions and falling projectiles#25% chance to reflect enemy shots
synergies: Dr. Fetus / Epic Fetus / Ipecac: Isaac becomes immune to his explosives.
Walnut: Walnut will only require 1-5 explosions to open. As Isaac is immune to explosions, he can safely open Walnut without taking any damage.

collectibles.name: Restock
desc_lines: Buying an item from a Shop restocks it instantly
Restocked items increase in price each time
collectibles.desc_raw: Buying an item from a Shop restocks it instantly#Restocked items increase in price each time
synergies: 2 of Diamonds + Blank Card
Coins can be infinitely generated by buying Lil' Battery. As Lil' Batteries only cost 5 coins, as long as Isaac starts with at least 11, the maximum of 99 coins can be quickly reached.
This cannot be repeated infinitely outside of Greed Mode, as Restock now increases the price of a shop slot each time something is purchased from it.
Deep Pockets allows this to once again be repeated infinitely, as the price will cap at 99 coins, while up to 999 coins can be obtained.
nr Steam Sale x2: Items can be infinitely bought from shops.
Blank Card + 2 of Diamonds: nr If a Lil' Battery is on sale, items can be bought infinitely.
r If a Lil' Battery is on sale, Isaac can buy a lot of items before the prices become too high in that shop.
Chaos: Restock is affected by Chaos, and can be used to obtain otherwise rare items.
nr Chaos + Steam Sale x2: Items can be bought infinitely, allowing Isaac to theoretically obtain every item in the game.

Keeper's Box: Buying spawned pickup or item in Shop will always restock into the same thing as what being sold in the upper left of the shop and their prices will never increase. This still works even if the top left slot already brought and allowed to change the top left spot with restock machine to items or your desired pickups. This does not work outside of the shop.
Member Card: Items bought in the separate shop will also be restocked.
A Pound of Flesh: Restocked items in a shop will still require hearts to be bought.
If playing as The Lost, Tainted Lost, or if currently affected by Soul of the Lost / White Fire Place, items can be bought indefinitely.

collectibles.name: Bursting Sack
desc_lines: Spider enemies no longer target or deal contact damage to Isaac
collectibles.desc_raw: Spider enemies no longer target or deal contact damage to Isaac

collectibles.name: No. 2
desc_lines: Holding a fire button for 2.35 seconds spawns a lit Butt Bomb
collectibles.desc_raw: Holding a fire button for 2.35 seconds spawns a lit Butt Bomb

collectibles.name: Pupula Duplex
desc_lines: ↑ x2 Tear size
Spectral tears
collectibles.desc_raw: ↑ {{Tearsize}} x2 Tear size#Spectral tears
synergies: Godhead: The damaging aura benefits from the increased size significantly.

collectibles.name: Pay To Play
desc_lines: +5 Coins
Locked blocks, doors and chests must be opened with coins instead of keys
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Coin}} +5 Coins#Locked blocks, doors and chests must be opened with coins instead of keys

collectibles.name: Eden's Blessing
desc_lines: ↑ +0.7 Tears
Grants a random item at the start of the next run
collectibles.desc_raw: ↑ {{Tears}} +0.7 Tears#Grants a random item at the start of the next run

collectibles.name: Friendly Ball
desc_lines: Can be thrown at enemies to capture them
Using the item after capturing an enemy spawns the capture as a friendly companion
Walking over the ball after a capture instantly recharges the item
collectibles.desc_raw: Can be thrown at enemies to capture them#{{Friendly}} Using the item after capturing an enemy spawns the capture as a friendly companion#Walking over the ball after a capture instantly recharges the item
synergies: The Battery: Walking over a captured enemy will double charge the ball. This allows Isaac the ability to release a captured enemy and catch a new one in the same room double charging the ball to release and capture more enemies. Attempting to catch a new enemy while the old one is in the process of spawning will result in the ball phasing through the new enemy and wasting the charge.
Blood Puppy: Using the ball on an angered Blood Puppy will instantly tame it. Using the ball again results in a purely cosmetic blood explosion.

collectibles.name: Tear Detonator
desc_lines: Splits all of Isaac's tears currently on screen in a circle of 6 tears
collectibles.desc_raw: Splits all of Isaac's tears currently on screen in a circle of 6 tears
synergies: Akeldama: Will bust tears generated by Akeldama into Isaac's current tears.
Angelic Prism: Tears that are split by the prism can be split again by Tear Detonator.
Anti-Gravity: All tears currently floating in the air will be multiplied, resulting in a massive amount of tears.
Bethany/Tainted Bethany: Bethany can use Tear Detonator multiple times in rapid succession at the cost of one Soul/Blood Charge per use, allowing for a huge area of effect that wipes out most rooms.
Car Battery: Splits tears twice, creating 36 tears for every tear currently airborne.
The Battery: Allows for multiple uses, creating tear counts in the hundreds.
Circle of Protection / Metal Plate: Reflected tears can be split.
Compound Fracture/Cricket's Body/The Parasite: Tears that are split can split again into burst tears.
Eye of the Occult: Split tears form a circle around the original tear's position, and can be controlled as normal.

Haemolacria: Splits tears into 6 Haemolacria tears that burst into split tears. If split tears are burst, all of them will split into 6 Haemolacria tears creating an enormous amount of tears when the main tear splits again.
Hushy: All 8 projectiles shot by Hushy will split and become Isaac's tears, gaining their effects.
Ipecac: All tears will split into 6 explosive tears, making it very powerful (but also dangerous unless Isaac has Host Hat or Pyromaniac).

Lachryphagy: Similar to the Haemolacria synergy, if the main tear splits, 6 tears will spawn that can be fed. However, if burst tears are split, all burst tears will split into 6 tears resulting in an enormous amount of tears that are likely to feed each other.
Lead Pencil: Each of the tears explodes into 6 smaller tears.
The Ludovico Technique: The tear explodes into six smaller tears, then reappears next to the player.
My Reflection: The shrapnel tears will curve back to the location of the original detonated tear.
Sad Bombs: Bomb tears are multiplied.
Saturnus: Orbiting tears can be split, including enemy tears caught in the orbit.
Shooting familiars: Shooting familiars' tears explode into Isaac's tears, with his actual damage and effects.
Technology Zero: Tears generated by Tear Detonator can be connected with electric charges.
Technology Zero + Mucormycosis / Sinus Infection: Spores/Boogers generated by Tear Detonator can be connected with electric charges.
Tiny Planet: Tears split, and then orbit around where the tear was originally from, three spinning clockwise, three counterclockwise.
Trisagion: Tears split into six beams.
Uranus: Tears shot out from frozen enemies when they are destroyed can be split.
Urn of Souls: Each flame splits into six tears, unlike other fire effects.

collectibles.name: Lil Gurdy
desc_lines: Launches and bounces around the room with speed based on charge amount
Deals 5-20 contact damage per hit depending on speed
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Chargeable}} Launches and bounces around the room with speed based on charge amount#Deals 5-20 contact damage per hit depending on speed

collectibles.name: Bumbo
desc_lines: Picks up nearby coins
Levels up after getting 6, 12, and 24 coins
Lv2: Chance to spawn item after room clears
Lv3: Shoots tears that can spawn coins on hit
Lv4: Chases enemies, occasionally dropping bombs, can spawn item on coin pickup
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Coin}} Picks up nearby coins#Levels up after getting 6, 12, and 24 coins#Lv2: Chance to spawn item after room clears#Lv3: Shoots tears that can spawn coins on hit#Lv4: Chases enemies, occasionally dropping bombs, can spawn item on coin pickup
synergies: BFFS!: Doubles both the contact and shot damage, when applicable.

collectibles.name: D12
desc_lines: Rerolls any obstacle into another random obstacle (e.g. poop, pots, TNT, red poop, stone blocks etc.)
Small chance to reroll obstacles into buttons, killswitches, crawlspaces or trapdoors
collectibles.desc_raw: Rerolls any obstacle into another random obstacle (e.g. poop, pots, TNT, red poop, stone blocks etc.)#Small chance to reroll obstacles into buttons, killswitches, crawlspaces or trapdoors
synergies: Lucky Rock, Meconium, Petrified Poop, and Stud Finder can provide extra value in conjunction with their associated obstacles.
Leo / Mega Mush / Notched Axe / Pyro / Stompy / Sulfuric Acid / Terra / Thunder Thighs can provide Isaac a way to destroy the re-rolled obstacles.
r Car Battery: Re-rolls have a higher chance of entering the looping pool and creating obstacles that can't reroll (Reward Plates, Killswitches, Trap doors and Crawl Spaces)

XVI - The Tower? / A Card Against Humanity: Created obstacles can be rerolled into more useful objects such as Rainbow Poop or Golden Poop. Rocks created from XVI - The Tower? will have a larger pool to pick from.

collectibles.name: Censer
desc_lines: Familiar surrounded by a huge aura of light that slows down enemies and projectiles in it
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Slow}} Familiar surrounded by a huge aura of light that slows down enemies and projectiles in it
synergies: 2Spooky / Venus: The slowing aura mitigates the delay between fast enemies that charge Isaac and the status effect, making it much harder for Isaac to take contact damage.
Bot Fly / Psy Fly: The slowing aura allows these items to deal with the incoming projectiles more reliably.
The Soul: The slowing aura allows incoming enemies and shots to be more reliably deflected.

collectibles.name: Key Bum
desc_lines: Picks up nearby keys
Spawns random chests in return
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Key}} Picks up nearby keys#{{Chest}} Spawns random chests in return
synergies: BFFS!: Familiar is bigger and drops chests more often.
Flat Penny: Chests frequently contain pennies, which have a chance of spawning more keys for Key Bum to pick up, possibly lead to a loop.
Gilded Key / The Left Hand: Works as expected.
Guppy's Eye: Allows Isaac to choose which Locked Chests are worth opening and which to pass, thus saving keys. Great for min-maxing.

collectibles.name: Rune Bag
desc_lines: Spawns a random rune or Soul Stone every 7-8 rooms
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Rune}} Spawns a random rune or Soul Stone every 7-8 rooms
synergies: BFFS!: The drop rate is improved to every 4-5 rooms.
r The drop rate is improved to every 6 rooms.

collectibles.name: Seraphim
desc_lines: Shoots Sacred Heart tears
Deals 10 damage per tear
collectibles.desc_raw: Shoots Sacred Heart tears#Deals 10 damage per tear
synergies: Sacrificial Altar: This familiar will also reward 2 Black Hearts when sacrificed.

collectibles.name: Betrayal
desc_lines: Enemies can hit each other with their projectiles, and start infighting
collectibles.desc_raw: Enemies can hit each other with their projectiles, and start infighting

collectibles.name: Zodiac
desc_lines: Grants a random zodiac item effect every floor
collectibles.desc_raw: Grants a random zodiac item effect every floor
collectibles.unlock: Defeat Ultra Greed as The Lost

collectibles.name: Serpent's Kiss
desc_lines: 15% chance to shoot poison tears
Poison enemies on contact
Enemies killed by contact poison have a chance to drop a Black Heart on death
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Poison}} 15% chance to shoot poison tears#{{Poison}} Poison enemies on contact#{{BlackHeart}} Enemies killed by contact poison have a chance to drop a Black Heart on death
synergies: nrDr. Fetus / Epic Fetus: Bombs and missiles may become poisonous, and enemies hit by them may drop black hearts.
r Bombs no longer grant black hearts but poison bombs leave a poison cloud similar to Bob's Curse.
Gnawed Leaf: Standing still allows for safe kills and possibly spawn infinite black hearts.
Mars: Dashing into enemies will often both kill enemies, and cause them to drop black hearts.
nrMom's Knife: Enemies poisoned by the knife may drop black hearts.
Tainted Forgotten: Especially strong as you are likely to apply contact poison when attacking enemies, giving you black hearts frequently.
Any item or card that grants Isaac temporary invincibility lets Isaac use Serpent's Kiss on any enemy without taking damage. This includes, but is not limited to
VII - The Chariot/VII - The Chariot?
A Pony/White Pony
Book of Shadows/Celtic Cross/Empty Vessel/Algiz/The Polaroid/Virgo
Breath of Life
Dark Arts using the very brief period after reappearing where Isaac is immune to damage
Dull Razor
Holy Mantle
My Little Unicorn/Unicorn Stump/Feels like I'm walking on Sunshine!
rAries (if walking speed is at least 0.85)
The Gamekid/Power Pill!
Ventricle Razor (briefly after exiting a portal)

collectibles.name: Marked
desc_lines: ↑ +0.7 Tears
↑ +3 Range
Isaac automatically shoots tears at a movable red target on the ground
Familiars shoot towards the target too
You can stop shooting and reset the target's location by pressing the drop button ()
collectibles.desc_raw: ↑ {{Tears}} +0.7 Tears#↑ {{Range}} +3 Range#Isaac automatically shoots tears at a movable red target on the ground#Familiars shoot towards the target too#You can stop shooting and reset the target's location by pressing the drop button ({{ButtonRT}})
synergies: r Brimstone: Automatically charges and fires in the direction of the mark. The angle the beam is shot from Isaac will correct itself when the mark or Isaac repositions to continue hitting the mark.
Camo Undies: Isaac will stay camouflaged until the crosshair is moved. If you control Marked with the mouse (only possible on PC) Camo Undies will stay camouflaged.

Chocolate Milk: Charge and fire rate based on proximity to mark. Fully charges when farther away, charges less but fires faster when closer. Damage scales with the distance between Isaac and the crosshair.
Cursed Eye: Greatly increases the firing rate without needing to charge. The teleporting effect of the eye is removed.
r Lil Brimstone: While firing, Lil Brimstone automatically charges and fires the beam immediately upon full charge, the beam will now target the crosshair.
Maw Of The Void/Montezuma's Revenge: While firing, these items charge automatically and activate immediately upon full charge.
r Mom's Knife: Charge and fire rate based on proximity to mark. Fully charges when farther away, charges less but fires faster when closer.
r Monstro's Lung: Automatically charges and fires in the direction of the mark.
Pointy Rib: The rib familiar will aim at the crosshair.
Quints: The spawned familiars will aim at the crosshair.
Revelation: Light beams are charged and fired at the crosshair automatically at a fixed angle from Isaac.
Rocket in a Jar: Bombs placed will blast at the crosshair, and cannot be placed normally until the crosshair disappears.
r Tech X: Fires in the direction of the crosshair, with charging time proportionate to the distance of the crosshair from Isaac.
r Tractor Beam: The beam is aimed at Marked's crosshair. Moving the crosshair rotates the entire beam along with any tears inside it.

collectibles.name: Tech X
desc_lines: Isaac's tears are replaced by a chargeable laser ring
Ring size and damage increases up to 100% with charge time
collectibles.desc_raw: Isaac's tears are replaced by a chargeable laser ring#Ring size and damage increases up to 100% with charge time
synergies: 20/20: Fires two Tech X rings instead of one. rDamage down applied.
Apple!/Euthanasia/Holy Light/Little Horn/Mucormycosis/Tough Love: The tear effects will apply randomly at each damage tick depending on Isaac's luck stat.
Azazel: Azazel fires a Brimstone ring instead of Tech X ring with a 35% damage penalty. If combined with Brimstone, the damage penalty is negated.
The same applies to Tainted Azazel.
Bookworm: Can fire an additional Tech X ring with same 25% chance.
Brimstone: Fires a Brimstone ring. This Brimstone ring is larger than the laser ring and has a much wider hitbox.
nrBrimstone + Technology: The damage boost from Brimstone + Technology is lost, but the laser fires while charging the Brimstone ring.
r Fires a Brimstone ring coiled by a laser. Once again increases Isaac's damage by 1.5x.
r Double Brimstone: Further increases the width of the ring, and grants +1 Damage.
r Cricket's Body: Shoots out smaller tears on hit.
C Section: Overrides the charging effect, but shoots a laser ring with a fetus tear in the middle. The ring's damage is half the fetus' damage.
Dr. Fetus: Overrides the charging effect, but shoots a laser ring with a bomb in the middle. The ring's damage is Isaac's Damage.
Epic Fetus: Each missile shoots 10 laser beams like from Technology in each direction with a flat 25 damage bonus, similar to Tammy's Head.
Explosivo/Nose Goblin/Sinus Infection: Every damage tick has a chance to stick a tear of the respective effect on enemies.
Fruit Cake: Works on the laser rings.
Haemolacria: Haemolacria tears will burst into 3-5 Tech X rings on impact. The sizes of the rings are various, but they deal 100% Isaac's Damage.
Haemolacria + Brimstone: Brimstone overrides the effects of Tech X, effectively making it useless.

Head of the Keeper: The tears turn into the Tech X tears, but they still retain the ability for enemies to drop money, which is now based on every tick of damage, rather than with the rings themselves.
Homing: While the rings won't home in on enemies, they will bend inwards to hit enemies within each ring, resulting in a substantial damage area increase.
Jacob's Ladder: The rings gain a light blue tint and generate sparks when hitting enemies or obstacles. A ring can generate only one spark per tick.
Lead Pencil: Every 15th laser ring fired also shoots a cluster of tears.
Loki's Horns: Adds a chance to fire 4 laser rings at once with 2 in front and 2 behind Isaac, each pair firing on top of each other.
Lost Contact: Tech X rings can block projectiles as they travel.
Mom's Eye: Isaac will now sometimes fire an additional ring from his back.
Mom's Knife: Simultaneously fires the knife and laser ring(s).

collectibles.name: Ventricle Razor
desc_lines: Creates up to two portals to travel between
Can be placed in different rooms
collectibles.desc_raw: Creates up to two portals to travel between#Can be placed in different rooms
synergies: Tainted Magdalene: She can abuse the invincibility from the stacked portals to let enemies approach her and die in her hugshttps://youtu.be/vRYclaFycH0.

collectibles.name: Tractor Beam
desc_lines: ↑ +1 Fire rate
↑ +1 Fire rate cap
↑ +2.5 Range
↑ +0.16 Shot speed
Isaac's tears always travel along a beam of light in front of him
collectibles.desc_raw: ↑ {{Tears}} +1 Fire rate#↑ {{Tears}} +1 Fire rate cap#↑ {{Range}} +2.5 Range#↑ {{Shotspeed}} +0.16 Shot speed#Isaac's tears always travel along a beam of light in front of him
synergies: 20/20/Bookworm/Conjoined/The Inner Eye/Keeper/Mutant Spider/Tainted Keeper/The Wiz: Every extra tear will follow the beam instead of having a spread, making it easier to hit enemies with the extra tears. Essentially increases damage per second.
Compound Fracture: All split tears will follow the beam greatly increasing effective damage.
a+nr Bone tears will not burst into bone shards when fired at obstacles, however, bone tears will burst into bone shards when fired at enemies.
r Bone tears burst into bone shards when fired at obstacles.
Cricket's Body/The Parasite: All split tears will follow the beam greatly increasing effective damage.
Continuum: The tears will re-appear directly in front of Isaac and continue their flight path after going off screen.
Guillotine: The beam will visually follow Isaac's head as it orbits, but tears will still stay centered relative to Isaac's body making his tears easier to aim.
Lachryphagy: Tears can still be fed and will still burst. nr Burst tears will travel along the beam.
Monstro's Lung: All tears are fired along the beam but at different heights.
Ouroboros Worm: The altered patterns tears travel in is confined to the beam allowing Isaac to benefit from the stat bonus and spectral tears granted without losing accuracy.
Pop!: Tears stick along the tractor beam even when they slow down and collide, capable of creating a dense line of shots.
Rainbow Worm: The altered patterns tears travel in is confined to the beam allowing Isaac to benefit from the stat bonuses granted without losing accuracy.
r Many of the worm trinket effects it gives now grant spectral tears as well.
Ring Worm: The altered pattern tears travel in is confined to the beam allowing Isaac to benefit from its stat bonus and spectral tears without losing accuracy.
Rubber Cement / Super Ball: Tears will no longer be bound to the beam after bouncing off of something.
Sinus Infection: Once the booger falls off the enemy, Isaac can loosely control it by moving and firing in different directions, and make it stick to another enemy.
Tear copiers like Cain's Other Eye / Incubus / Sumptorium / Twisted Pair: Tears shot by these familiars will originate from Isaac, following the beam.
Wiggle Worm: The altered pattern tears travel in is confined to the beam allowing Isaac to benefit from its stat bonus without losing accuracy.
r Wiggle Worm also grants spectral tears with little downside in accuracy.

collectibles.name: God's Flesh
desc_lines: Tears can shrink enemies
Shrunken enemies can be crushed and killed by walking over them
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Shrink}} Tears can shrink enemies#Shrunken enemies can be crushed and killed by walking over them
synergies: Compound Fracture / Cricket's Body / The Parasite: Split tears will retain shrinking effect if main tear had shrinking effect. With Compound Fracture and The Parasite, tears with a shrinking effect look like normal tears.
Finger!: Finger can also apply the shrinking effect.
Lachryphagy: Will occasionally fire tears with shrinking effect. All burst tears have a chance to apply the shrinking effect.
The Ludovico Technique: Tear flickers blue, capable of shrinking enemies when it does.
Technology Zero: Electricity connected to tears with shrinking effect has a chance to shrink enemies.

collectibles.name: Maw of the Void
desc_lines: Firing tears for 2.35 seconds and releasing the fire button creates a black brimstone ring around Isaac
It deals 30x Isaac's damage over 2 seconds
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Chargeable}} Firing tears for 2.35 seconds and releasing the fire button creates a black brimstone ring around Isaac#It deals 30x Isaac's damage over 2 seconds
synergies: Maw Of The Void, Number Two, Montezuma's Revenge and Revelation all have the same charge time, allowing Isaac to easily utilize them at same time.
Dark Arts: Extremely powerful. Isaac will unleash a black Brimstone ring while using Dark Arts, letting Isaac do crazy damage while invincible.

Guppy: Damaging enemies with Maw Of The Void allows flies to spawn from the Guppy transformation. Flies are spawned at a rate of approximately one fly per tick of damage for each enemy it hits. This causes a lot of flies to spawn very quickly. The spawned flies will also move to attack the nearest enemy immediately.

Hemoptysis: Enemies hit by the sneeze will gain a curse that increases the damage the black ring deals to 2x and +3 flat damage. Enemies who die to the black ring under the curse will damage and spread the curse to nearby enemies.
Homing tears: The ring curves towards enemies.
Ipecac: Massively increases ring damage and poisons enemies.
r The ring also explodes on enemy or obstacle contact.
Jacob's Ladder: The ring sends out electric currents when it hits something.
Lost Contact: Ring can destroy projectiles.
Mysterious Liquid: Occasionally spawns a pool of creep where the ring makes contact with an enemy.
Pisces/8 Inch Nails: Ring obtains knockback effect.
Proptosis: na+ The ring is much larger and deals 1.5x damage per tick.
a+nr The ring is larger but deals normal damage.
r No effect.
Strange Attractor: Ring attracts pickups and enemies towards it.
Wiggle Worm: The ring has wavy edges.

collectibles.name: Spear of Destiny
desc_lines: Isaac holds a spear in front of him
The spear deals twice his damage and can fear enemies on contact
collectibles.desc_raw: Isaac holds a spear in front of him#{{Fear}} The spear deals twice his damage and can fear enemies on contact

collectibles.name: Explosivo
desc_lines: 25% chance to shoot sticky tears
Sticky tears grow and explode after a few seconds, dealing Isaac's damage +60
collectibles.desc_raw: 25% chance to shoot sticky tears#Sticky tears grow and explode after a few seconds, dealing Isaac's damage +60
synergies: rBrimstone/The Ludovico Technique/Mom's Knife/Tech X: Every tick of damage Isaac’s tears deal have a chance to attach a separate Explosivo tear on a hit enemy.

Cricket's Body: The explosion results in split Explosivo shots being generated. nr If the target survives, they're likely to immediately stick again. r The split tears explode when they hit an enemy or the ground.
r Dr. Fetus: Dr. Fetus bombs may occasionally turn into Sticky Bombs, but don't spawn spiders or slowing white creep.
Fire Mind: Explosivo tears leave a flame behind after exploding.

Godhead: The tear keeps its damaging aura while attached to the enemy, the aura grows as the tear does until it explodes. The aura can damage only enemies other than the one the tear is stuck to, however.

Haemolacria: Main tears and burst tears can have the Explosivo effect. If a main tear has the Explosivo effect, it will burst with its explosion, the burst tears dealing damage based off the explosion damage.
Ipecac: Sticky shots will explode even if they fall to the floor after the initial target dies.
Lachryphagy: Explosivo tears can be fed and will burst into Explosivo tears; however, if normal tears are fed first, Explosivo fed to the normal tears will not give the tear the Explosivo effect.
Lost Contact: Tears that stick to enemies can still block/be destroyed by enemy shots.

r Shielded Explosivo tears are no longer destroyed by shots while they're stuck to enemies. Up to the point that they explode, they block all enemy shots that come into contact with them, effectively cutting off an entire direction of attack (much like Dry/Fart Baby, but with a larger area of blockage and without forcing the player to waste time positioning). As such, this is an extremely powerful synergy between otherwise adequate items.
Mr. Mega: Increases the damage bonus of Explosivo's explosions to 110.
Mysterious Liquid: Leaves a pool of green creep on the floor.
Parasitoid: Egg sacs that trigger the explosion will leave a larger pool of slowing creep.
Nose Goblin/Sinus Infection: Explosivo tears can also be booger tears which damage the enemy over time while stuck to them before exploding.
Soy Milk: The 60 point explosion damage bonus is not affected by Soy Milk's penalty, combining efficiently with the high fire rate.
Spirit Sword: The fired projectile can take on Explosivo's effects. The sword swing can leave behind Explosivo tears.
Strange Attractor: When a tear sticks to an enemy, nearby enemies will be quickly pulled in the direction of the tear. The constant pull may however make enemy movement unpredictable.
rTech.5: The laser also has a chance to apply explosive tears on enemies.
rTechnology: Regular explosive tears may stick to enemies hit by the laser.
rTechnology 2: The laser has a chance to apply an explosive tear every tick. The 60 point explosion damage bonus is not affected by the laser's penalty, combining efficiently with the high tick rate.
Technology Zero: Stuck tears will still generate arcs of electricity. Multiple tears stuck to a single enemy can cause very rapid damage.
Trisagion: Every tick of damage Isaac’s tears deal have a chance to attach a separate Explosivo tear on a hit enemy.

synergies: Astral Projection: During the time-stop period of Astral Projection, there is a chance that the eyes fired will stop in the air instead of flying in a direction, and will last indefinitely until an enemy bump into it, greatly increase the existing time of evil eyes. Triggering of Astral Projection also greatly increase fire rate, giving a higher possibility to fire eyes.
Ghost Pepper / Bird's Eye: Eye tears won't shoot fires. Instead, they make Isaac shoots fire more frequently.
Pause: After using pause, eyes will remain frozen in place even after pause's effect has ran out.
Homing tears: Eyes will fire homing tears; however, the eye itself will not home in on enemies.
Mom's Eye / Loki's Horns: Evil Eye has a chance to be shot in the corresponding direction. r Eyes shot in other directions will still fire in the direction Isaac is shooting.

Tear Detonator: All tears generated have a chance to be eyes. Eyes themselves do not detonate into six tears.

synergies: Meat Cleaver: Both of the smaller enemies produced by Meat Cleaver add to the damage bonus, allowing rooms with few enemies to grant much more damage.

synergies: Dull Razor: Dull Razor counts towards the payout despite not removing health from Isaac.
Hypercoagulation: By intentionally taking damage with something like a fire and grabbing the half-red hearts, Isaac can easily generate powerful familiars for free.

Scapular: By intentionally taking damage with something like a fire to half a red heart a soul heart will be generated. Leaving the room and taking damage again will continuously allow damage to be taken.

collectibles.name: More Options
desc_lines: Allows Isaac to choose between 2 items in treasure rooms
collectibles.desc_raw: {{TreasureRoom}} Allows Isaac to choose between 2 items in treasure rooms
synergies: Crooked Penny / Diplopia: Both items are copied, allowing Isaac to take both copied items without the other disappearing. The original pedestals will function as normal, disappearing the other if one is collected.
Devil's Crown: Both items can be taken, although they must be paid for with hearts.

Glowing Hourglass: Entering an unexplored More Options Treasure Room and then using Glowing Hourglass can cause the room's layout to change rerolling the offered items. The reroll is not always guaranteed as Glowing Hourglass is causing the Treasure Room to alternate between a set number of different More Options room layouts on a given floor with repeat layouts offering the same items.
Jacob and Esau: Both brothers can take one item each if they are lined up properly.
Pay to Win: Treasure Rooms will always contain a Shop Restock Machine in addition to the two choice items.
In Downpour, Dross, and Mines, Pay to Win by itself can sometimes spawn the combined Treasure Rooms, even without More Options. Having both doesn't guarantee the combined effect.
For Ashpit, Mausoleum, Gehenna, and Corpse, this combination does work correctly for normal-sized Treasure Rooms, but not thin ones.

collectibles.name: Crown Of Light
desc_lines: +2 Soul Hearts
If Isaac has no damaged heart containers:

↓ -0.3 Shot speed
Taking any damage removes the effect for the room
collectibles.desc_raw: {{SoulHeart}} +2 Soul Hearts#If Isaac has no damaged heart containers:#↑ {{Damage}} x2 Damage multiplier#↓ {{Shotspeed}} -0.3 Shot speed#Taking any damage removes the effect for the room
synergies: Holy Mantle: This will block damage and keep Crown Of Light active.
Rock Bottom: While the diamond tear effect will disappear when Isaac is hit, the damage up will not be reverted.

synergies: A Dollar: Allows Isaac to gain the full 100 coins when picking this up.
Contract From Below / Guppy's Tail: If the room clear reward is removed, spawns 1-3 random coins. Allows Deep Pockets to remain useful at high luck.
Greed's Gullet: After exceeding 100 coins, Isaac gains an extra Heart Container for every 100 coins instead of every 25.
Keeper/Tainted Keeper: The limit of 12 heart containers can be bypassed, allowing for up to 15 coin hearts at 999 coins. Additional coin hearts past 12 are not displayed.
Money Power: Increases maximum damage bonus. The damage caps at +39.96 damage with 999 coins.
Midas' Touch: Increases maximum contact damage. The damage caps at 203.3 damage with 999 coins.
Options?: A pickup, card, pill, trinket, or Chest will accompany each coin.

↑ x1.5 Damage multiplier while standing in the aura
collectibles.desc_raw: Bounces around the room surrounded by a damaging aura that deals 7.5-10 damage per second#↑ {{Damage}} x1.5 Damage multiplier while standing in the aura
synergies: Rock Bottom: The damage boost is permanent. Damage is easier to stack, since Isaac can enter the Succubus aura to permanently boost any temporary damage-ups.

collectibles.name: Fruit Cake
desc_lines: Each one of Isaac's tears gets a different effect
collectibles.desc_raw: Each one of Isaac's tears gets a different effect
synergies: Dr. Fetus: Bombs pull their effects from bomb modifiers (Bobby-Bomb, Bob's Curse, Bomber Boy, Butt Bombs, Glitter Bombs, Hot Bombs, Mr. Mega, Sad Bombs, Scatter Bombs, Sticky Bombs) as well as tear effects.
Finger!/Technology 2/Brimstone: Each tick has a random effect, allowing it to rapidly afflict enemies with multiple status effects.
This also includes the Sulfuric Acid effect to destroy rocks and enter secret rooms.
Isaac's Tears/Tammy's Head/Tear Detonator: Each tear will have its own tear effect.
The Ludovico Technique: The tear will cycle through different effects, based on Isaac's tear rate.

If Isaac stays in one room long enough the Ludovico tear will often fill the room with many stationary Trisagion tears that last until Isaac leaves the room. This combination can easily clear most rooms.
Mom's Knife: The benefits of fruit cake still occur but are short lived due to the high damage of Mom's knife.
Trisagion: Each Trisagion tear only has one effect with each tick having a chance to trigger the same effect.

Grants bigger explosions
Fireplaces explode when extinguished

synergies: Ventricle Razor: When the Ventricle Razor portals are placed on the opposite wall of a room and Isaac goes from one portal to another repeatedly, a pentagram symbol can be created which covers almost the whole room.
How to Jump: Jumping can create large pentagrams by drawing a rectangle.
Spin to Win: Faster movement speed while using spin to win allows for larger pentagrams to be created.

collectibles.name: Kidney Bean
desc_lines: Charms all enemies in close range
collectibles.desc_raw: {{Charm}} Charms all enemies in close range
synergies: Bird's Eye/Ghost Pepper: Whenever the player creates a charming fart, a fire will be shot in a random direction.
Second Hand: Doubles charm duration.

collectibles.name: Glowing Hourglass
desc_lines: Brings Isaac back to the previous room and reverses all actions done in the room the item was used in
The rewind can be used three times per floor
Acts as The Hourglass when out of rewinds, which slows enemies down for 8 seconds
collectibles.desc_raw: Brings Isaac back to the previous room and reverses all actions done in the room the item was used in#The rewind can be used three times per floor#{{Collectible66}} Acts as The Hourglass when out of rewinds, which slows enemies down for 8 seconds
synergies: Any reroll item/collectible: Items in the room can be rerolled, and if the result is undesirable, then Isaac can go back in time to save whatever was used to reroll. However, note that activating the reroll on the same items again will result in the same items being generated as the first time. Rerolling different items from the same type of room (such as going to a different Shop) will result in the reroll effect creating new and different items. This technique is useful for saving charges on items like The D6, or getting multiple options with consumables like the Perthro.

Anything that spawns an item such as Mystery Gift / XVII - The Stars?: After the player activates them, they can then go back in time if the item is undesirable. However, unlike reroll effects, using something that spawns an item/collectible will always generate that same item, dependent on the room type, even if the player goes to a different one in the future. For example, activating XVII - The Stars? in a Secret Room will create two items from the Secret Room item pool, and even if the player goes to the next floor and uses it again in a new Secret Room, the same exact items will be generated. So, the best way to use something that spawns an item is to activate it in rooms with different item pools (Secret, Devil/Angel, Treasure, Shop, etc.) and see which item would be the best before accepting it.
na+ ? Card: Allows for infinite usage as the ? Card gets refunded upon usage.
nr 9 Volt: Clearing any room is enough to instantly recharge the hourglass, allowing infinite tries for the best outcome.
na+The Battery: When the item is double charged and used, it will also refund the used extra charge bar, making it possible to use it endlessly.
a+nr48 Hour Energy pill: Allows for infinite usage as the pill gets refunded upon use.
a+nrSharp Plug: Allows for infinite usage as long as you have more than 2 full hearts as the hearts used to charge will be refunded upon use.
a+nrHabit: Allows rooms to be repeated infinitely as long as Isaac has enough health to take two hits, which are refunded on use.
Hairpin: If Hairpin is held while entering any Boss Room for the first time, Glowing Hourglass may be used indefinitely on said room to repeat the fight until you beat it.
Moving Box: Using the Glowing Hourglass after retrieving it from Moving Box will not deplete its charge.
Curse of the Tower: If used quickly after taking damage, Troll bombs will spawn in the previous room. If you have the Safety Scissors, these can be harvested as normal.

Crooked Penny + Schoolbag: If there is a battery and an item in the same room, you can multiply items an infinite number of times. Every time you fail the Crooked Penny flip, you can activate the Glowing Hourglass, go back in, grab a battery, then try again. Every time you succeed a flip, you must leave the room and enter it again to "save" your progress.
rnr+ More Options: If used after entering the Treasure Room in an Alt-Path floor, the items in the room will change.

r If Tainted Eden's active item re-rolls into Glowing Hourglass, it can be used immediately to return Tainted Eden to their previous state. This allows the player to have practically infinite attempts to keep their loadout of passive items as long as they travel back in time before being hit again.
While playing as Tainted Eden and rerolling an active item into the Glowing Hourglass, you can infinitely spawn wisps as much as possible until you reroll it again.

Duality: The doors to the Devil Room and Angel Room can stay open if you use the Glowing Hourglass inside their rooms, so Duality lets you check the items of both of them before deciding which room to use, provided two charges are still available by the end of the floor.

Forget Me Now + Schoolbag: Using Forget Me Now will fully recharge Glowing Hourglass on the new floor, using Glowing Hourglass after that will take Isaac back to the previous room while also giving back Forget Me Now. This allows for infinite usage of Glowing Hourglass as well as infinite retries of any room as long as Forget Me Now is used before the room is cleared.

Saturnus: Using the Glowing Hourglass will activate the effect of Saturnus again, essentially allowing for double the amount of orbiting tears, if the player returns to a room, where the tears created by Saturnus were not destroyed.

r If activated while under the effect of Soul of Azazel, as long as you had the consumable in the previously visited room, you will keep the Megablast effect while having another copy of the soul in your inventory.
Schoolbag + Mega Blast + Glowing Hourglass: Works the same way

Sack Head: Coins spawned from hitting enemies have a chance to become sacks.

r Technology: The laser is yellow and has a chance to drop a penny upon damaging an enemy.
Technology Zero: Coin tears are connected via electricity. Beams of electricity can cause enemies to drop coins.
Technology 2: Due to Technology 2's constant fire rate, enemies are hit many more times than usual, resulting in a higher likelihood of coins dropping.

Sprinkler: Tears fired by the sprinkler can be duplicated.
Technology: When the laser passes through Multidimensional Baby, it doubles the laser.

Tainted Azazel: Enemies hit by the sneeze will gain a curse that increases the damage the laser deals to 10x and +3 flat damage (instead of just 5x). Enemies who die to the laser under the curse will damage and spread the curse to nearby enemies.
Hemoptysis: The sneeze can be activated while Mega Blast is firing.
Schoolbag: laser will continue to fire when switching to another active item. Other active items can be used while the laser is firing.

Haemolacria: Fires a barrage of Haemolacria tears, all of which will burst.
Lachryphagy: Tears tend to clump together, becoming one giant tear which splits into 8 large tears. When tears burst, they can also spawn barrages of tears that can be fed.
Pop!: Causes burst tears to remain in the room for much longer, forming a soft barrier of sorts, which enemies must survive through to reach Isaac.
Torn Card: Decreases shot requirement by 1. Lead Pencil will fire every 14th shot, with Torn Card quickly following at 15th.
Trisagion: Fires a barrage of beams.
The Scooper: All tears benefit from the damage bonus granted by The Scooper.
Sprinkler: Sprinkler tears count for activation of the Lead Pencil effect. The tear burst may fire from the sprinkler or Isaac.
Flat Stone: Tears fired by Lead Pencil will bounce, greatly increasing their range.

synergies: Chocolate Milk: How much the 20th tear was charged determines the size and damage of the golden tear.
Compound Fracture / Cricket's Body / The Parasite: Golden tears will still look like coins and will still split into other golden coins when hitting obstacles.
Equality!: The coin lost when firing the tear can be quite useful for equalizing the pickup counter.
Humbleing Bundle: Affects coins dropped from hitting enemies, allowing Isaac to potentially farm money.
Lachryphagy: Golden tears can also be fed and will burst into split tears. Burst tears are not golden.
Piercing tears: The golden tear will petrify all enemies it hits, r and will drop a penny for each one.
Trisagion: Golden tears become blast that constantly drop Pennies on hit.
Urn of Souls: Golden tears still shoot as normal while using the urn.

C Section: Fetuses are skeletons that shoot splitting bone fragments when in contact with an enemy.
Cricket's Body/The Parasite: Each tear splits into more tears, which will all shatter.

Haemolacria: Tears will sometimes burst into smaller tears when hitting obstacles or enemies. These smaller tears are miniature versions of Haemolacria tears, which will burst when hitting the ground.
Ipecac: If the bone tear hits a wall or an obstacle, it will split into explosive half-damage shards. Hits on enemies and the floor behave as normal Ipecac.
The Ludovico Technique: Tear turns into a spinning bone that rapidly spews out smaller bone shots on contact with enemies.
Kidney Stone: First bullet shatters into smaller kidney stone bullets.
Spectral tears: Bones pass through obstacles and generate shards for each obstacle they pass through, which can result in many shards being generated from a single bone.
Technology Zero: The bone fragments will be connected by the electric current.

collectibles.desc_raw: 20% chance to shoot a sticky booger#{{Damage}} Boogers deal Isaac's damage once a second and stick for 10 seconds#{{Luck}} Not affected by luck
synergies: Brimstone/The Ludovico Technique/Tech X/Trisagion: Every tick of damage Isaac’s tears deal have a chance to attach a separate booger tear on a hit enemy.
Cricket's Body: Booger tears will split into split booger tears that can attach to enemies.
Explosivo: Explosivo tears can also be booger tears which damage the enemy over time while stuck to them before exploding.

Godhead: Shots retain their aura while stuck to an enemy, but the aura can damage only enemies other than the booger victim. If the enemy dies before the booger’s maximum duration, it will resume homing and will often stick to a second enemy.
Guppy: Damage instances from boogers attached to enemies have a 100% (r 50%) chance to spawn a Blue Fly.

Lachryphagy: All tears have a chance to burst into boogers. Boogers will always burst into more boogers, though some of the boogers may incorrectly appear to be normal tears. When a booger sticks to an enemy, it will burst into 8 boogers which have a high chance of sticking again to the same enemy.
Lost Contact: Boogers that stick to enemies will block shots, and will not be destroyed in the process.
Polyphemus: Booger shots lose the Polyphemus pierce effect, but stick to enemies while dealing the full damage of the Polyphemus shot. Damage does not degrade.
Spirit Sword: Swings and projectiles have a chance to apply boogers on enemies.
Tech.5: The laser also has a chance to apply boogers on enemies.
Technology: Hit enemies have a chance for a booger to be applied to them.
Technology 2: The laser can also apply boogers with the laser’s chance to apply boogers being determined rapidly every tick.
Technology Zero: Boogers can be connected with electricity while being attached dealing large amounts of damage to the booger victim.

synergies: C Section: Parasitoid fetuses spawn spiders/flies and slowing creep with every hit on an enemy.
Compound Fracture / Cricket's Body / The Parasite: When egg sacks are fired, they will split into smaller egg sacks. Split egg sacks from The Parasite will not look like egg sacks.
Cupid's Arrow: Egg sacks will apply their effect to every enemy hit.
Fire Mind / Explosivo : If the explosion effect triggers on an egg sack, it will produce a much larger pool of slowing creep.
Hive Mind: Flies and spiders are bigger and deal double damage.
Technology 2: Rapid damage ticks can cause a large amount of flies and spiders to spawn.

collectibles.name: Eye of Belial
desc_lines: ↑ +1.5 Range
Piercing tears
Hitting an enemy makes the tear homing and doubles its damage
collectibles.desc_raw: ↑ {{Range}} +1.5 Range#Piercing tears#Hitting an enemy makes the tear homing and doubles its damage
synergies: Compound Fracture/Cricket's Body/The Parasite: Tears that pass through enemies will become Belial tears and split into smaller Belial tears. Non-Belial split tears can also turn into Belial tears if they pass through enemies.
C Section: Fetuses will continue to deal damage per tick after gaining the effect, almost doubling total damage output.
Godhead: Normal tears and Eye of Belial tears will retain damaging aura.
Lachryphagy: Eye of Belial tears can still be fed and will burst, and burst Eye of Belial tears will still be homing. Tears from regular bursts can also turn into Eye of Belial tears.
Lost Contact: Piercing through a projectile triggers the double damage and homing effect.
Remote Detonator: Placed Bombs won't explode automatically, so they can be placed and have tears fired through them to activate double damage and homing.

rTiny Planet: After piercing an enemy, tears will continue orbiting rather than homing, but each tear can gain multiple damage and size multipliers. These tears can make quick work of even most bosses.

Saturnus: When an orbiting tear hits an enemy, it will turn into an Eye of Belial tear. From that point, the tear gets out of Saturnus' orbit every time an enemy is nearby, becomes autonomous and chases every enemy in the room. It won't be destroyed by enemies anymore. When there's no enemy close from it, it comes back into the orbit. As all 7 tears can become Eye of Belial ones, this synergy is significantly powerful.
Technology Zero: All tears will be connected with electricity. Electricity will double in damage when the tears change.

Since The Jar will store any leftover health from healing Red Heart Containers, this can allow for potentially infinite health when healing half-empty containers. Especially effective in conjunction with Cancer or The Wafer.

synergies: Mom's Box: Doubles the range increase.
Golden Trinket / Mom's Box: Having either makes the spiral pattern larger and doubles the stat increase. Having both makes the pattern even larger and triples the stat increase.
Aquarius: Creep has a chance to be homing.
Dr. Fetus: Since the bombs don't follow the spiral pattern, only the extra range, spectral bombs, and chance for a homing bomb apply.

trinkets.name: Nose Goblin
desc_lines: 10% chance to shoot a sticky tear
50% chance for it to be homing
Boogers deal Isaac's damage once per second
Boogers stick for 10 seconds
trinkets.desc_raw: 10% chance to shoot a sticky tear#50% chance for it to be homing#{{Damage}} Boogers deal Isaac's damage once per second#Boogers stick for 10 seconds
synergies: rBrimstone/The Ludovico Technique/Tech X/Trisagion: Every tick of damage Isaac’s tears deal have a chance to attach a separate booger tear on a hit enemy.
Cricket's Body: Booger tears will split into split booger tears that can attach to enemies.
Explosivo: Explosivo tears can also be booger tears which damage the enemy over time while stuck to them before exploding.

Godhead: Shots retain their aura while stuck to an enemy, but the aura can damage only enemies other than the booger victim. If the enemy dies before the booger’s maximum duration, it will resume homing and will often stick to a second enemy.
Golden Trinket / Mom's Box: Having either raises the chance to fire a booger tear to 20% chance and to 75% the chance for it to be homing. Having both raises the chance to respectively 30% and 100%.
Guppy: Damage instances from boogers attached to enemies have a 100% (r 50%) chance to spawn a Blue Fly.

Lachryphagy: All tears have a chance to burst into boogers. Boogers will always burst into more boogers, though some of the boogers may incorrectly appear to be normal tears. When a booger sticks to an enemy, it will burst into 8 boogers which have a high chance of sticking again to the same enemy.
Lost Contact: Boogers that stick to enemies will block shots, and will not be destroyed in the process.
Polyphemus: Booger shots lose the Polyphemus pierce effect, but stick to enemies while dealing the full damage of the Polyphemus shot. Damage does not degrade.
Sinus Infection: The 50% homing also applies to boogers fired by Sinus Infection.
Spirit Sword: Swings and projectiles have a chance to apply boogers on enemies.

Strange Attractor: Attraction effect will remain on the booger shot stuck to an enemy. This can drag the enemy across the whole room until they reach one wall and get stuck on it, effectively disabling their normal movement.
Tech.5: The laser also has a chance to apply boogers on enemies.
Technology: Hit enemies have a chance for a booger to be applied to them.
Technology 2: The laser can also apply boogers with the laser’s chance to apply boogers being determined rapidly every tick.
Technology Zero: Boogers can be connected with electricity while being attached dealing large amounts of damage to the booger victim.

↑ +0.5 Speed
↑ +0.5 Tears
↑ +1.5 Damage
↑ +1.5 Range

synergies: 4.5 Volt: Dim Bulb will be active for as long as the active item has less than one bar of charge.
Bethany / Tainted Bethany: The effect remains active with both soul and blood charges.

Golden Trinket / Mom's Box: Having either doubles the stat increases. Having both triples them.

↑ +2 Tears

Options?: While Equality! is active, if one of the two rewards are doubled pickups, both rewards can be collected without the other despawning. Options? also makes Equality! easier to keep active.
Tainted ???: Tainted ??? will always have 0 bombs, making the trinket only possible to activate while also having 0 coins and keys (except with the Bombs are Key pill).
Tainted Keeper: Doubled pennies from killed enemies will not despawn. All other coin types will still disappear.

Holy Mantle: Explosions hitting the mantle still count for the trinket.
Host Hat / Pyromaniac: Isaac becomes immune to explosions, which allows to get the effect without any risk.

More Options: Treasure Rooms will always contain two choice items in addition to the Shop Restock Machine.
In Downpour, Dross, and Mines, Pay to Win by itself can sometimes spawn the combined Treasure Rooms, even without More Options. Having both doesn't guarantee the combined effect.
For Ashpit, Mausoleum, Gehenna, and Corpse, this combination does work correctly for normal-sized Treasure Rooms, but not thin ones.

synergies: Golden Trinket / Mom's Box: Each copy will spawn one more locust.
Bomber Boy: Additional smaller explosions will spawn in a tight cross formation.
Brimstone Bombs: The locust explosions fires thinner beams, dealing 4.8 damage per tick.
Glitter Bombs: Locust explosions have a chance to spawn pickups or trinkets.
Hive Mind: The locusts’ impact damage is doubled and the explosion damage is increased to 85.
Sad Bombs: A ring of 10 tears is shot outward upon detonation, which are affected by Isaac's tear modifiers, and deal damage equal to 15 + 0.6x, where x is Isaac's Damage.

r+ Only one item will drop.

synergies: Golden Trinket / Mom's Box: Having either makes all farts gain knockback. Having both makes all farts gain knockback and inflict confusion.
Contagion: All enemies killed while infected produce a much larger gas cloud, making the spread much more effective.
Isaac's Heart: Increases the knockback amount and radius of the heart when fully charged and the heart makes farting noises when it shoots.
Jupiter: Affects all gas clouds produced, increases them in size drastically.
Linger Bean: The clouds will be larger, increasing the range drastically. The damage is unaffected.
Mucormycosis: Spore explosions count as farts, and increase in size.

trinkets.name: A Lighter
desc_lines: Entering a room has a 20% chance to burn random enemies
Removes Tick

synergies: Golden Trinket / Mom's Box: At base luck, having either increases the chance to 33% and having both to 50%.
Tick: Tick can be removed by picking up A Lighter.

trinkets.name: Broken Padlock
desc_lines: Doors, key blocks and golden chests can be opened with explosions
Explosions can also open the "Strange Door" in "Depths II"
trinkets.desc_raw: Doors, key blocks and golden chests can be opened with explosions#Explosions can also open the "Strange Door" in "Depths II"
synergies: Golden Trinket / Mom's Box: Challenge rooms, arcades and Mega Satan door can be opened with bombs. Allows to open boss rooms with bombs as well if Isaac has both Golden Trinket and Mom's Box.

Notched Axe: Allows the Notched Axe to open locked chests and doors. The opening of chests does not use durability; however, when opening locked doors, only every other door does not use durability for the first four doors opened on the current floor. If Isaac uses the axe's durability or puts away the axe, the other door uses durability to open.

Options?: Bombing any chest spawned by Options?, including the ones not requiring keys, allows picking up the contents of the chest without the other reward disappearing, unless the empty chest is touched. This can be easily avoided by simply exiting and reentering the room.
Sulfuric Acid / Terra: Locked doors can be opened by shooting at them.

synergies: Golden Trinket / Mom's Box / Myosotis: Each copy increases the amount of pickups spawned by 2, up to a maximum of 8 pickups spawned per floor.

desc_lines: Using an active item rerolls it
trinkets.desc_raw: Using an active item rerolls it

trinkets.name: Teardrop Charm

trinkets.desc_raw: {{Luck}} +4 Luck towards luck-based tear effects
synergies: Golden Trinket / Mom's Box: Each copy gives an additional +2 Luck flat to the trinket.

Effect may consume hearts needed for healing

Hypercoagulation / Soul of Magdalene / Tainted Magdalene: Temporary hearts can be turned into blue spiders.
Options?: Converting hearts dropped by Options? will not cause the other consumable to disappear.

desc_lines: 2x Fire rate for familiars
trinkets.desc_raw: 2x Fire rate for familiars

trinkets.unlock: Complete the Boss Rush as Bethany
synergies: Glowing Hourglass: Wisps given by Beth's Faith will persist through the time leap to the upper floor, as well as giving new ones after going back down to the next floor.
Golden Trinket / Mom's Box: Gives 8 wisps at once.

trinkets.name: Old Capacitor
desc_lines: Prevents active item from charging by clearing a room
Clearing a room has a 20% chance to spawn a battery
33% chance at 5 luck
trinkets.desc_raw: {{Battery}} Prevents active item from charging by clearing a room#{{Battery}} Clearing a room has a 20% chance to spawn a battery#{{Luck}} 33% chance at 5 luck
synergies: Golden Trinket / Mom's Box: The chance for a battery dropping is always 33%.
4.5 Volt: Since 4.5 Volt also makes it so that active items don't charge when clearing rooms, this trinket can be picked up with no downside.

Dim Bulb : Since items do not automatically recharge upon being used, being able to hold both Old Capacitor and Dim Bulb together will allow the latter's effects to be constantly active, while the batteries allow control over when exactly to charge active items.
Jacob's Ladder / 120 Volt: Allows zaps to arc on the same enemy multiple times.
Options?: Also offers alternate rewards for the extra batteries.

trinkets.name: Brain Worm
desc_lines: Tears turn 90 degrees to target enemies that they may have missed
trinkets.desc_raw: Tears turn 90 degrees to target enemies that they may have missed
synergies: Golden Trinket / Mom's Box: Having either makes shots have a 25% chance to pierce. Having both increase the chance to 50%.

trinkets.name: Perfection
desc_lines: ↑ +10 Luck up
Taking damage destroys the trinket
trinkets.desc_raw: ↑ {{Luck}} +10 Luck up#Taking damage destroys the trinket
synergies: Blanket / Dogma / Holy Card / Holy Mantle / The Lost / Soul of the Lost / White Fire Places / Wooden Cross: Losing the shield does not destroy Perfection.
Blood Oath / Dull Razor / Sharp Plug / IV Bag: Does not destroy Perfection, nor does it prevent its acquisition.

Glowing Hourglass: Makes it much easier to hold on to Perfection since, as long as it can be used in the room where you take damage before you leave, Isaac can reset to the previous room with the trinket intact.

The Lost / Tainted Lost: Since these characters are likely to die instantly after taking a single hit, Perfection is almost guaranteed to drop after the third floor boss room is cleared and is also almost guaranteed to stay with them until their deaths.
This means getting luck based items at any point of the run, especially ones with luck scaling that tops out at about 10 luck, is a very good idea.
The ability to Smelt/Gulp trinkets will also add to the synergy, as there are many luck-based trinkets as well.

Rock Bottom: The +10 luck persists after switching trinkets as stats are locked to their highest values ever reached after picking Rock Bottom up. However, after losing Perfection, additional luck-ups will have no effect until the luck bonus lost is made up. (The stats are still being calculated behind the scenes, but until it surpasses the run's post-rock-bottom luck record, the record will be used instead).

trinkets.name: Devil's Crown
desc_lines: Treasure Room items are replaced with devil deals
trinkets.desc_raw: {{RedTreasureRoom}} Treasure Room items are replaced with devil deals
synergies: r+ Golden Trinket / Mom's Box: Having either makes treasure rooms have a 25% chance of becoming a Number Magnet variant. Having both increases the chance to 33%.
Coupon: When used in a Devil Treasure Room, gives the item for free, acting like a regular Treasure Room but with devil items, only requiring the use of Coupon.
This doesnt affect the Angel Room chance, like how using Coupon in a regular Devil Room and grabbing the free item wouldn't.
Dream Catcher: Shows you the item that spawns without the trinket, allowing you to drop the trinket before entering if you think the treasure room item would be better than the devil deal.
Glowing Hourglass: Allows you to check the item that spawns with and without the trinket, letting you pick the better item.
More Options: All Devil Treasure Rooms will have two items, both of which can be purchased.
This also extends to Broken Glasses, Golden Horse Shoe and XVII - The Stars cards buffed by Tarot Cloth.

trinkets.name: Charged Penny
desc_lines: Picking up a coin has a 17% chance to add 1 charge to the active item
Higher chance from nickels and dimes

trinkets.unlock: Donate to Battery Bums until they pay out with an item 5 times
synergies: Golden Trinket / Mom's Box: Each copy adds one more charge to Isaac's active item.

trinkets.desc_raw: All familiars orbit around Isaac

Spin to Win: Familiars made into orbitals by Friendship Necklace can deal contact damage and block enemy shots while Spin to Win is active.

trinkets.desc_raw: Right before taking damage, uses the active item if it is charged

trinkets.unlock: Defeat The Beast as Tainted Cain
synergies: Golden Trinket / Mom's Box: Having either makes the blue rooms drop double rewards when cleared. Having both triples the rewards.
Door Stop / Mercurius: The blue room can be exited without fighting the enemies, and the door will remain unlocked.

Planetariums can spawn in the Womb and Corpse

The die disappears when leaving
The die does not take up a pill/card slot
trinkets.desc_raw: 50% chance per new room to grant a single use die consumable item#The die disappears when leaving#The die does not take up a pill/card slot
synergies: Golden Trinket / Mom's Box: The chance to get a die increases to 100%.
Book of Virtues: Creates wisps as if the equivalent active item was used.

Glowing Hourglass: The die given in the room Isaac used Glowing Hourglass in will be granted in the next new room Isaac visits, regardless of what it is. This can be strategically used to get a D6 or Eternal D6 at the optimal moment.
Moving Box: Can be used to hold undesirable items and pickups until Dice Bag grants a consumable that can reroll them.

trinkets.name: Holy Crown

trinkets.name: Mother's Kiss
desc_lines: +1 Heart container while held
trinkets.desc_raw: {{Heart}} +1 Heart container while held
synergies: Golden Trinket / Mom's Box: Adds 1 more heart container per copy.
If the trinket is picked up while holding Mom's Box, 1 heart container will not be filled.
Guppy's Paw: The trinket will be consumed, but the soul hearts from conversion are kept.
Potato Peeler: The trinket will be consumed, but the damage and Cube of Meat is kept.

trinkets.name: Torn Card
desc_lines: Every 15 shots, Isaac shoots an Ipecac + My Reflection tear with a very high range value
trinkets.desc_raw: Every 15 shots, Isaac shoots an {{Collectible149}} Ipecac + {{Collectible5}} My Reflection tear with a very high range value
synergies: Golden Trinket / Mom's Box: Each copy reduces the number of shots required to shoot an ipecac tear by 5, with a minimum of 5 shots in between.
Pyromaniac: Completely negates the downside of Torn Card and will heal you if the shot misses an enemy or wall and hits you instead.
Host Hat: Completely negates the downside of Torn Card.
Almond Milk / Soy Milk / Chocolate Milk: Unlike the normal Ipecac synergy, Torn Card shots ignore the damage down multiplier, always dealing +40 damage, significantly increasing DPS.
Brain Worm: Brain Worm forces some tears to snap 90 degrees to target enemies that they may have missed.
Death's Touch / Pupula Duplex: Ipecac shots get a bigger hitbox, making shots easier to hit, and from a safer distance.
Eye of the Occult: Tear control is stronger than the reflection, although it's still present if Isaac stops shooting. Effectively negates downside.
Fire Mind: A fire is always spawned at the place of explosion.
Homing: The homing is stronger than the reflection, effectively negating Torn Card's downside.
Hook Worm: Hook Worm allows tears to hit enemies more easily, while also making them easier to avoid.
Lead Pencil: Lowers tear shot requirement by 1. Lead Pencil will always proc at the 14th shot, soon followed by Torn Card's at 15th.
Monstro's Lung: An Ipecac shot is fired for 4 bursts consecutively, with the 5th burst lacking one, and repeat. Makes Torn Card very consistent.
Proptosis: Ipecac tear will always explode before reflection takes effect. It's the only item that affects Torn Card's flat 40 damage.
Spirit Sword: Swings can be spammed rapidly to create Ipecac shots.
Tiny Planet: Ipecac shots will stick to the orbit and direction Isaac is aiming.
Whip Worm: Increased shot speed boosts accuracy and the tear is more likely to hit walls if the enemy is missed.

trinkets.name: Torn Pocket
desc_lines: Taking damage makes Isaac drop 2 of his coins, keys or bombs
The pickups can be replaced with other variants, such as golden keys, nickels, dimes, etc.
trinkets.desc_raw: Taking damage makes Isaac drop 2 of his coins, keys or bombs#The pickups can be replaced with other variants, such as golden keys, nickels, dimes, etc.
trinkets.unlock: Defeat Isaac, ???, Satan, and The Lamb as Tainted Lazarus
synergies: Golden Trinket / Mom's Box: Each copy makes Isaac drop 1 more pickup from his inventory.
Penny Trinkets : This can be used to generate extra pickups as long as one has a coin.
BOGO Bombs: Dropped bombs will be their doubled variants.
Humbleing Bundle: All dropped pickups have a chance to be replaced with doubled variants.
Keeper / Tainted Keeper: Grants a chance to drop coins upon taking damage, giving back the lost health.
Scapular: Can be used to infinitely generate pickups when down to half a Heart by re-entering a room to regenerate a Soul Heart.

trinkets.name: Gilded Key

Replaces all chests (except Old/Mega) with golden chests
Golden chests can contain extra cards, pills or trinkets

synergies: Golden Trinket / Mom's Box: +10% chance for chests as room rewards per copy.
If Isaac has both a golden trinket and the Box, the chance is increased by +20%.
Paper Clip: Allows all Golden Chests spawned to be opened without a key.

Sharp Key: Enemies killed by keys will only give the contents of locked chests allowing items to be obtained through Sharp Key more consistently. The pickups they can drop will also include runes and Lil' Battery.

desc_lines: Clearing a room has a 33% chance to turn the player into Azazel
Effect lasts until clearing and leaving another room

synergies: Golden Trinket / Mom's Box / multiple copies: One boost or additional copy increases the chance of Isaac turning into Azazel to 50%. Further boosts or copies increase the chance to 100%.
Rock Bottom: Isaac retains the damage multiplier and base speed increase after the effect ends. Also removes the tear delay multiplier when transformed into Azazel.
